With an unseekable\n79\t\t// body the Go transport falls back to Transfer-Encoding: chunked and drops\n80\t\t// Content-Length (AWS accepts that, B2 does not), so spool to a temp file\n81\t\t// to give the transport a seekable body with a known length.\n82\t\tif _, ok := body.(io.ReadSeeker); !ok {\n83\t\t\ttmp, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-put-*\")\n84\t\t\tif err != nil {\n90\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n91\t\t\t}\n92\t\t\tif _, err := tmp.Seek(0, io.SeekStart); err != nil {\n93\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: aws.String(key),\n100\t\t\tBody:        body,\n101\t\t\tContentLength: aws.Int64(size),\n102\t\t\tMetadata:    toS3Metadata(meta),\n103\t\t})\n104\t\tif err != nil {\n105\t\t\treturn for free egress.\n112\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Get(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n113\t\t// First get metadata via HeadObject (direct to B2, no egress url.PathEscape(key))\n153\t\n154\t\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, \"GET\", cfURL, nil)\n155\t\tif err != nil {\n156\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create request: %w\", err)\n157\t\t}\n158\t\n159\t\t// Set Range header\n160\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", offset, offset+length-1))\n161\t\n162\t\tresp, err := b.httpClient.Do(req)\n163\t\tif err != nil {\n164\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cloudflare request failed: %w\", err)\n165\t\t}\n166\t\n167\t\tif resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusPartialContent {\n168\t\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n169\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cloudflare returned status %d\", resp.StatusCode)\n170\t\t}\n171\t\n172\t\t// Extract relevant headers\n173\t\theaders := make(map[string]string)\n174\t\tif cfStatus := resp.Header.Get(\"CF-Cache-Status\"); cfStatus != \"\" {\n175\t\t\theaders[\"CF-Cache-Status\"] = cfStatus\n176\t\t}\n177\t\tif cfRay := resp.Header.Get(\"CF-Ray\"); cfRay != \"\" {\n178\t\t\theaders[\"CF-Ray\"] = cfRay\n179\t\t}\n180\t\tif age := resp.Header.Get(\"Age\"); age != \"\" {\n181\t\t\theaders[\"Age\"] = age\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\treturn resp.Body, headers, nil\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t// Head retrieves object metadata without the body.\n188\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Head(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\n189\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\n190\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n191\t\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n192\t\t})\n193\t\tif err != nil {\n194\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HeadObject failed: %w\", err)\n195\t\t}\n196\t\n197\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n198\t\t\tKey:          key,\n199\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n200\t\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n201\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n202\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n203\t\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n204\t\t}\n205\t\n206\t\t// Check if this is an ARMOR-encrypted object\n207\t\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n208\t\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n209\t\t\t// Use plaintext size from metadata\n210\t\t\tif am, _ := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n211\t\t\t\tinfo.Size = am.PlaintextSize\n212\t\t\t}\n213\t\t}\n214\t\n215\t\treturn info, nil\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// HeadVersion retrieves object metadata for a specific version.\n219\t// Returns ARMOR metadata (plaintext size) for the specific version.\n220\tfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadVersion(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, versionID string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\n221\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\n222\t\t\tBucket:    aws.String(bucket),\n223\t\t\tKey:       aws.String(key),\n224\t\t\tVersionId: aws.String(versionID),\n225\t\t})\n226\t\tif err != nil {\n227\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HeadObject failed: %w\", err)\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n231\t\t\tKey:          key,\n232\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n233\t\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n234\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n235\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n236\t\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\t// Check if this is an ARMOR-encrypted object\n240\t\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n241\t\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n242\t\t\t// Use plaintext size from metadata\n243\t\t\tif am, _ := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n244\t\t\t\tinfo.Size = am.PlaintextSize\n245\t\t\t}\n246\t\t}\n247\t\n248\t\treturn info, nil\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// Delete removes an object from B2.\n252\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Delete(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) error {\n253\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObject(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectInput{\n254\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n255\t\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n256\t\t})\n257\t\tif err != nil {\n258\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteObject failed: %w\", err)\n259\t\t}\n260\t\treturn nil\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// DeleteObjects removes multiple objects from B2.\n264\tfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteObjects(ctx context.Context, bucket string, keys []string) error {\n265\t\tobjects := make([]types.ObjectIdentifier, len(keys))\n266\t\tfor i, key := range keys {\n267\t\t\tobjects[i] = types.ObjectIdentifier{Key: aws.String(key)}\n268\t\t}\n269\t\n270\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObjects(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectsInput{\n271\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n272\t\t\tDelete: &types.Delete{\n273\t\t\t\tObjects: objects,\n274\t\t\t},\n275\t\t})\n276\t\tif err != nil {\n277\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteObjects failed: %w\", err)\n278\t\t}\n279\t\treturn nil\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\t// List objects in a bucket with optional prefix.\n283\tfunc (b *B2Backend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\n284\t\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n285\t\t\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\n286\t\t\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\n287\t\t\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\n288\t\t}\n289\t\tif delimiter != \"\" {\n290\t\t\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\n291\t\t}\n292\t\tif maxKeys > 0 {\n293\t\t\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\n294\t\t}\n295\t\n296\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\n297\t\tif err != nil {\n298\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\", err)\n299\t\t}\n300\t\n301\t\tresult := &ListResult{\n302\t\t\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n303\t\t\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\n304\t\t}\n305\t\n306\t\t// Process objects\n307\t\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\n308\t\t\t// Filter out .armor/ internal objects\n309\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(aws.ToString(obj.Key), \".armor/\") {\n310\t\t\t\tcontinue\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\n313\t\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n314\t\t\t\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\n315\t\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\n316\t\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\n317\t\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\n318\t\t\t}\n319\t\n320\t\t\t// Try to get ARMOR metadata (requires additional HeadObject call)\n321\t\t\t// For efficiency in listings, we rely on the plaintext size being\n322\t\t\t// stored in the object's metadata which requires HeadObject per item.\n323\t\t\t// For now, we return the raw size; callers needing plaintext size\n324\t\t\t// should use HeadObject individually.\n325\t\n326\t\t\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\n327\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\t// Process common prefixes\n330\t\tfor _, prefix := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\n331\t\t\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(prefix.Prefix))\n332\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\treturn result, nil\n335\t}\n336\t\n337\t// Copy copies an object in B2, supporting cross-bucket copy.\n338\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Copy(ctx context.Context, srcBucket, srcKey, dstBucket, dstKey string, meta map[string]string, replaceMetadata bool) error {\n339\t\tinput := &s3.CopyObjectInput{\n340\t\t\tBucket:     aws.String(dstBucket),\n341\t\t\tCopySource: aws.String(srcBucket + \"/\" + srcKey),\n342\t\t\tKey:        aws.String(dstKey),\n343\t\t}\n344\t\n345\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n346\t\t\tinput.MetadataDirective = types.MetadataDirectiveReplace\n347\t\t\tinput.Metadata = toS3Metadata(meta)\n348\t\t}\n349\t\n350\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.CopyObject(ctx, input)\n351\t\tif err != nil {\n352\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"CopyObject failed: %w\", err)\n353\t\t}\n354\t\treturn nil\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// GetDirect retrieves an object directly from B2 (not via Cloudflare).\n358\t// Used for metadata operations where we don't need the body.\n359\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetDirect(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n360\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{\n361\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n362\t\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n363\t\t})\n364\t\tif err != nil {\n365\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"GetObject failed: %w\", err)\n366\t\t}\n367\t\n368\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n369\t\t\tKey:          key,\n370\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n371\t\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n372\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n373\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n374\t\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n378\t\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n379\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\treturn resp.Body, info, nil\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// ComputeETag computes an MD5 ETag for the given data.\n385\tfunc ComputeETag(data []byte) string {\n386\t\thash := md5.Sum(data)\n387\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// ComputeETagReader computes an MD5 ETag from a reader.\n391\tfunc ComputeETagReader(r io.Reader) (string, error) {\n392\t\thash := md5.New()\n393\t\tif _, err := io.Copy(hash, r); err != nil {\n394\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n395\t\t}\n396\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash.Sum(nil)), nil\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\t// toS3Metadata converts a map to S3 metadata format.\n400\tfunc toS3Metadata(meta map[string]string) map[string]string {\n401\t\tif meta == nil {\n402\t\t\treturn nil\n403\t\t}\n404\t\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n405\t\tfor k, v := range meta {\n406\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n407\t\t}\n408\t\treturn result\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t// fromS3Metadata converts S3 metadata to a regular map.\n412\tfunc fromS3Metadata(meta map[string]string) map[string]string {\n413\t\tif meta == nil {\n414\t\t\treturn nil\n415\t\t}\n416\t\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n417\t\tfor k, v := range meta {\n418\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n419\t\t}\n420\t\treturn result\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t// ListRaw lists objects without filtering .armor/ internal objects.\n424\t// Used for internal operations such as manifest discovery where all keys\n425\t// under a prefix (including .armor/ prefixed keys) must be enumerated.\n426\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListRaw(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\n427\t\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n428\t\t\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\n429\t\t\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\n430\t\t\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\n431\t\t}\n432\t\tif delimiter != \"\" {\n433\t\t\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\n434\t\t}\n435\t\tif maxKeys > 0 {\n436\t\t\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\n437\t\t}\n438\t\n439\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\n440\t\tif err != nil {\n441\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\", err)\n442\t\t}\n443\t\n444\t\tresult := &ListResult{\n445\t\t\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n446\t\t\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\n450\t\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n451\t\t\t\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\n452\t\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\n453\t\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\n454\t\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\n455\t\t\t}\n456\t\t\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\n457\t\t}\n458\t\n459\t\tfor _, p := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\n460\t\t\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(p.Prefix))\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\treturn result, nil\n464\t}\n465\t\n466\t// S3Client returns the underlying S3 client for advanced operations.\n467\tfunc (b *B2Backend) S3Client() *s3.Client {\n468\t\treturn b.s3Client\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t// IsRetryableError checks if an error is retryable.\n472\tfunc IsRetryableError(err error) bool {\n473\t\tif err == nil {\n474\t\t\treturn false\n475\t\t}\n476\t\t// Check for common retryable conditions\n477\t\terrStr := err.Error()\n478\t\treturn strings.Contains(errStr, \"5xx\") ||\n479\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"503\") ||\n480\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"502\") ||\n481\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"timeout\") ||\n482\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"connection reset\")\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// ListBuckets lists all buckets.\n486\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListBuckets(ctx context.Context) ([]BucketInfo, error) {\n487\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListBuckets(ctx, &s3.ListBucketsInput{})\n488\t\tif err != nil {\n489\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListBuckets failed: %w\", err)\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\tbuckets := make([]BucketInfo, len(resp.Buckets))\n493\t\tfor i, bucket := range resp.Buckets {\n494\t\t\tbuckets[i] = BucketInfo{\n495\t\t\t\tName:         aws.ToString(bucket.Name),\n496\t\t\t\tCreationDate: aws.ToTime(bucket.CreationDate),\n497\t\t\t}\n498\t\t}\n499\t\n500\t\treturn buckets, nil\n501\t}\n502\t\n503\t// CreateBucket creates a new bucket.\n504\tfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n505\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.CreateBucket(ctx, &s3.CreateBucketInput{\n506\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n507\t\t})\n508\t\tif err != nil {\n509\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"CreateBucket failed: %w\", err)\n510\t\t}\n511\t\treturn nil\n512\t}\n513\t\n514\t// DeleteBucket deletes an empty bucket.\n515\tfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n516\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteBucket(ctx, &s3.DeleteBucketInput{\n517\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n518\t\t})\n519\t\tif err != nil {\n520\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteBucket failed: %w\", err)\n521\t\t}\n522\t\treturn nil\n523\t}\n524\t\n525\t// HeadBucket checks if a bucket exists.\n526\tfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n527\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.HeadBucket(ctx, &s3.HeadBucketInput{\n528\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n529\t\t})\n530\t\tif err != nil {\n531\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"HeadBucket failed: %w\", err)\n532\t\t}\n533\t\treturn nil\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// ReaderAtCloser wraps a reader with Close and ReadAt methods.\n537\ttype ReaderAtCloser struct {\n538\t\tdata []byte\n539\t}\n540\t\n541\t// NewReaderAtCloser creates a ReaderAtCloser from a byte slice.\n542\tfunc NewReaderAtCloser(data []byte) *ReaderAtCloser {\n543\t\treturn &ReaderAtCloser{data: data}\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\n547\t\tif len(r.data) == 0 {\n548\t\t\treturn 0, io.EOF\n549\t\t}\n550\t\tn = copy(p, r.data)\n551\t\tr.data = r.data[n:]\n552\t\treturn n, nil\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) ReadAt(p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error) {\n556\t\tif off >= int64(len(r.data)) {\n557\t\t\treturn 0, io.EOF\n558\t\t}\n559\t\tn = copy(p, r.data[off:])\n560\t\tif n < len(p) {\n561\t\t\terr = io.EOF\n562\t\t}\n563\t\treturn n, err\n564\t}\n565\t\n566\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Close() error {\n567\t\treturn nil\n568\t}\n569\t\n570\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Bytes() []byte {\n571\t\treturn r.data\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// CreateMultipartUpload initiates a multipart upload.\n575\tfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n576\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput{\n577\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n578\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n579\t\t\tMetadata: toS3Metadata(meta),\n580\t\t})\n581\t\tif err != nil {\n582\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n583\t\t}\n584\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.UploadId), nil\n585\t}\n586\t\n587\t// UploadPart uploads a part to a multipart upload.\n588\tfunc (b *B2Backend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n589\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.UploadPart(ctx, &s3.UploadPartInput{\n590\t\t\tBucket:        aws.String(bucket),\n591\t\t\tKey:           aws.String(key),\n592\t\t\tUploadId:      aws.String(uploadID),\n593\t\t\tPartNumber:    aws.Int32(partNumber),\n594\t\t\tBody:          body,\n595\t\t\tContentLength: aws.Int64(size),\n596\t\t})\n597\t\tif err != nil {\n598\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"UploadPart failed: %w\", err)\n599\t\t}\n600\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n601\t}\n602\t\n603\t// CompleteMultipartUpload completes a multipart upload.\n604\tfunc (b *B2Backend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n605\t\t// Convert to AWS SDK type\n606\t\tawsParts := make([]types.CompletedPart, len(parts))\n607\t\tfor i, p := range parts {\n608\t\t\tawsParts[i] = types.CompletedPart{\n609\t\t\t\tETag:       aws.String(p.ETag),\n610\t\t\t\tPartNumber: aws.Int32(p.PartNumber),\n611\t\t\t}\n612\t\t}\n613\t\n614\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput{\n615\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n616\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n617\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n618\t\t\tMultipartUpload: &types.CompletedMultipartUpload{\n619\t\t\t\tParts: awsParts,\n620\t\t\t},\n621\t\t})\n622\t\tif err != nil {\n623\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n624\t\t}\n625\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n626\t}\n627\t\n628\t// AbortMultipartUpload aborts a multipart upload.\n629\tfunc (b *B2Backend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n630\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput{\n631\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n632\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n633\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n634\t\t})\n635\t\tif err != nil {\n636\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"AbortMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n637\t\t}\n638\t\treturn nil\n639\t}\n640\t\n641\t// ListParts lists the parts of a multipart upload.\n642\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListParts(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) (*ListPartsResult, error) {\n643\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListParts(ctx, &s3.ListPartsInput{\n644\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n645\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n646\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n647\t\t})\n648\t\tif err != nil {\n649\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListParts failed: %w\", err)\n650\t\t}\n651\t\n652\t\tresult := &ListPartsResult{\n653\t\t\tBucket:           aws.ToString(resp.Bucket),\n654\t\t\tKey:              aws.ToString(resp.Key),\n655\t\t\tUploadID:         aws.ToString(resp.UploadId),\n656\t\t\tIsTruncated:      aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\tif resp.NextPartNumberMarker != nil {\n660\t\t\tif val, err := strconv.Atoi(*resp.NextPartNumberMarker); err == nil {\n661\t\t\t\tresult.NextPartNumberMarker = val\n662\t\t\t}\n663\t\t}\n664\t\n665\t\tfor _, part := range resp.Parts {\n666\t\t\tresult.Parts = append(result.Parts, PartInfo{\n667\t\t\t\tPartNumber:   aws.ToInt32(part.PartNumber),\n668\t\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(part.ETag),\n669\t\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(part.Size),\n670\t\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(part.LastModified),\n671\t\t\t})\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\treturn result, nil\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t// ListMultipartUploads lists active multipart uploads.\n678\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListMultipartUploads(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (*ListMultipartUploadsResult, error) {\n679\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListMultipartUploads(ctx, &s3.ListMultipartUploadsInput{\n680\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n681\t\t})\n682\t\tif err != nil {\n683\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListMultipartUploads failed: %w\", err)\n684\t\t}\n685\t\n686\t\tresult := &ListMultipartUploadsResult{\n687\t\t\tBucket:             aws.ToString(resp.Bucket),\n688\t\t\tIsTruncated:        aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n689\t\t\tNextKeyMarker:      aws.ToString(resp.NextKeyMarker),\n690\t\t\tNextUploadIDMarker: aws.ToString(resp.NextUploadIdMarker),\n691\t\t}\n692\t\n693\t\tfor _, upload := range resp.Uploads {\n694\t\t\tresult.Uploads = append(result.Uploads, UploadInfo{\n695\t\t\t\tUploadID:  aws.ToString(upload.UploadId),\n696\t\t\t\tKey:       aws.ToString(upload.Key),\n697\t\t\t\tInitiated: aws.ToTime(upload.Initiated),\n698\t\t\t})\n699\t\t}\n700\t\n701\t\treturn result, nil\n702\t}\n703\t\n704\t// GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration gets the lifecycle configuration for a bucket.\n705\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx context.Context, bucket string) ([]byte, error) {\n706\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx, &s3.GetBucketLifecycleConfigurationInput{\n707\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n708\t\t})\n709\t\tif err != nil {\n710\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration failed: %w\", err)\n711\t\t}\n712\t\n713\t\t// Build the XML response manually to ensure proper S3 format\n714\t\tvar rules []string\n715\t\tfor _, rule := range resp.Rules {\n716\t\t\truleXML := b.buildLifecycleRuleXML(rule)\n717\t\t\trules = append(rules, ruleXML)\n718\t\t}\n719\t\n720\t\txmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(`<LifecycleConfiguration xmlns=\"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\">%s</LifecycleConfiguration>`,\n721\t\t\tstrings.Join(rules, \"\"))\n722\t\treturn []byte(xmlBody), nil\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// buildLifecycleRuleXML builds XML for a single lifecycle rule.\n726\tfunc (b *B2Backend) buildLifecycleRuleXML(rule types.LifecycleRule) string {\n727\t\tvar parts []string\n728\t\n729\t\t// ID\n730\t\tif rule.ID != nil {\n731\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<ID>%s</ID>\", *rule.ID))\n732\t\t}\n733\t\n734\t\t// Status\n735\t\tif rule.Status != \"\" {\n736\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Status>%s</Status>\", rule.Status))\n737\t\t}\n738\t\n739\t\t// Filter\n740\t\tif rule.Filter != nil {\n741\t\t\tfilterXML := b.buildLifecycleFilterXML(rule.Filter)\n742\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Filter>%s</Filter>\", filterXML))\n743\t\t}\n744\t\n745\t\t// Expiration\n746\t\tif rule.Expiration != nil {\n747\t\t\tvar expParts []string\n748\t\t\tif rule.Expiration.Days != nil {\n749\t\t\t\texpParts = append(expParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Days>%d</Days>\", *rule.Expiration.Days))\n750\t\t\t}\n751\t\t\tif rule.Expiration.Date != nil {\n752\t\t\t\texpParts = append(expParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Date>%s</Date>\", rule.Expiration.Date.Format(time.RFC3339)))\n753\t\t\t}\n754\t\t\tif len(expParts) > 0 {\n755\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Expiration>%s</Expiration>\", strings.Join(expParts, \"\")))\n756\t\t\t}\n757\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\t// NoncurrentVersionExpiration\n760\t\tif rule.NoncurrentVersionExpiration != nil {\n761\t\t\tvar nveParts []string\n762\t\t\tif rule.NoncurrentVersionExpiration.NoncurrentDays != nil {\n763\t\t\t\tnveParts = append(nveParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<NoncurrentDays>%d</NoncurrentDays>\", *rule.NoncurrentVersionExpiration.NoncurrentDays))\n764\t\t\t}\n765\t\t\tif len(nveParts) > 0 {\n766\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<NoncurrentVersionExpiration>%s</NoncurrentVersionExpiration>\", strings.Join(nveParts, \"\")))\n767\t\t\t}\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\t// AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload\n771\t\tif rule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload != nil {\n772\t\t\tvar aimuParts []string\n773\t\t\tif rule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload.DaysAfterInitiation != nil {\n774\t\t\t\taimuParts = append(aimuParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<DaysAfterInitiation>%d</DaysAfterInitiation>\", *rule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload.DaysAfterInitiation))\n775\t\t\t}\n776\t\t\tif len(aimuParts) > 0 {\n777\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload>%s</AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload>\", strings.Join(aimuParts, \"\")))\n778\t\t\t}\n779\t\t}\n780\t\n781\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<Rule>%s</Rule>\", strings.Join(parts, \"\"))\n782\t}\n783\t\n784\t// buildLifecycleFilterXML builds XML for a lifecycle filter.\n785\tfunc (b *B2Backend) buildLifecycleFilterXML(filter *types.LifecycleRuleFilter) string {\n786\t\tif filter == nil {\n787\t\t\treturn \"\"\n788\t\t}\n789\t\n790\t\t// Check which field is set (only one can be set at a time)\n791\t\tif filter.Prefix != nil {\n792\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<Prefix>%s</Prefix>\", *filter.Prefix)\n793\t\t}\n794\t\tif filter.Tag != nil {\n795\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<Tag><Key>%s</Key><Value>%s</Value></Tag>\", aws.ToString(filter.Tag.Key), aws.ToString(filter.Tag.Value))\n796\t\t}\n797\t\tif filter.And != nil {\n798\t\t\tvar andParts []string\n799\t\t\tif filter.And.Prefix != nil {\n800\t\t\t\tandParts = append(andParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Prefix>%s</Prefix>\", *filter.And.Prefix))\n801\t\t\t}\n802\t\t\tfor _, tag := range filter.And.Tags {\n803\t\t\t\tandParts = append(andParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Tag><Key>%s</Key><Value>%s</Value></Tag>\", aws.ToString(tag.Key), aws.ToString(tag.Value)))\n804\t\t\t}\n805\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<And>%s</And>\", strings.Join(andParts, \"\"))\n806\t\t}\n807\t\treturn \"\"\n808\t}\n809\t\n810\t// PutBucketLifecycleConfiguration sets the lifecycle configuration for a bucket.\n811\tfunc (b *B2Backend) PutBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx context.Context, bucket string, config []byte) error {\n812\t\t// Parse the lifecycle configuration XML and convert to SDK types\n813\t\trules, err := b.parseLifecycleConfig(config)\n814\t\tif err != nil {\n815\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse lifecycle configuration: %w\", err)\n816\t\t}\n817\t\n818\t\t_, err = &types.BucketLifecycleConfiguration{Rules: rules},\n821\t\t})\n822\t\tif err != nil {\n823\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutBucketLifecycleConfiguration failed: %w\", err)\n824\t\t}\n825\t\treturn nil\n826\t}\n827\t\n828\t// parseLifecycleConfig parses lifecycle configuration XML into SDK types.\n829\tfunc (b *B2Backend) parseLifecycleConfig(config []byte) ([]types.LifecycleRule, error) {\n830\t\t// Simple XML parsing for common lifecycle rules\n831\t\t// This handles the most common cases; full XML parsing would use encoding/xml\n832\t\tvar rules []types.LifecycleRule\n833\t\n834\t\t// Use a simple approach: parse with encoding/xml\n835\t\ttype LifecycleConfiguration struct {\n836\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"LifecycleConfiguration\"`\n837\t\t\tRules   []struct {\n838\t\t\t\tID          string `xml:\"ID\"`\n839\t\t\t\tStatus      string `xml:\"Status\"`\n840\t\t\t\tPrefix      string `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n841\t\t\t\tFilter      *struct {\n842\t\t\t\t\tPrefix string `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n843\t\t\t\t} `xml:\"Filter\"`\n844\t\t\t\tExpiration *struct {\n845\t\t\t\t\tDays int `xml:\"Days\"`\n846\t\t\t\t} `xml:\"Expiration\"`\n847\t\t\t\tAbortIncompleteMultipartUpload *struct {\n848\t\t\t\t\tDaysAfterInitiation int `xml:\"DaysAfterInitiation\"`\n849\t\t\t\t} `xml:\"AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n850\t\t\t} `xml:\"Rule\"`\n851\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\tvar lc LifecycleConfiguration\n854\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(config, &lc); err != nil {\n855\t\t\treturn nil, err\n856\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\tfor _, r := range lc.Rules {\n859\t\t\trule := types.LifecycleRule{\n860\t\t\t\tID:     aws.String(r.ID),\n861\t\t\t\tStatus: types.ExpirationStatus(r.Status),\n862\t\t\t}\n863\t\n864\t\t\t// Handle filter\n865\t\t\tif r.Filter != nil && r.Filter.Prefix != \"\" {\n866\t\t\t\trule.Filter = &types.LifecycleRuleFilter{\n867\t\t\t\t\tPrefix: aws.String(r.Filter.Prefix),\n868\t\t\t\t}\n869\t\t\t} else if r.Prefix != \"\" {\n870\t\t\t\trule.Filter = &types.LifecycleRuleFilter{\n871\t\t\t\t\tPrefix: aws.String(r.Prefix),\n872\t\t\t\t}\n873\t\t\t}\n874\t\n875\t\t\t// Handle expiration\n876\t\t\tif r.Expiration != nil {\n877\t\t\t\trule.Expiration = &types.LifecycleExpiration{\n878\t\t\t\t\tDays: aws.Int32(int32(r.Expiration.Days)),\n879\t\t\t\t}\n880\t\t\t}\n881\t\n882\t\t\t// Handle abort incomplete multipart upload\n883\t\t\tif r.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload != nil {\n884\t\t\t\trule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload = &types.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload{\n885\t\t\t\t\tDaysAfterInitiation: aws.Int32(int32(r.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload.DaysAfterInitiation)),\n886\t\t\t\t}\n887\t\t\t}\n888\t\n889\t\t\trules = append(rules, rule)\n890\t\t}\n891\t\n892\t\treturn rules, nil\n893\t}\n894\t\n895\t// DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration deletes the lifecycle configuration for a bucket.\n896\tfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx context.Context, Parts must be at least 5MB\n16\t//     (except the last part) and part numbers must be >= 1.\n17\t//\n18\t//   CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts) -> finalETag\n19\t//     Completes a multipart upload by combining all uploaded parts. Parts must\n20\t//     be sorted by part number and include all uploaded parts with their ETags.\n21\t//\n22\t//   AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n23\t//     Cancels a multipart upload and releases all storage associated with it.\n24\t//     Should be called for cleanup when an upload fails.\n25\t//\n26\t// Helper Functions\n27\t//\n28\t// The MultipartUploadHelper provides higher-level helper functions for common\n29\t// multipart upload scenarios:\n30\t//\n31\t//   NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend, bucket) -> helper\n32\t//     Creates a helper for a specific bucket.\n33\t//\n34\t//   helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, metadata) -> uploadInfo\n35\t//     Creates a new multipart upload.\n36\t//\n37\t//   helper.UploadPart(ctx, uploadInfo, partNumber, data) -> error\n38\t//     Uploads a single part (includes validation).\n39\t//\n40\t//   helper.CompleteUpload(ctx, uploadInfo) -> finalETag\n41\t//     Completes the multipart upload.\n42\t//\n43\t//   helper.AbortUpload(ctx, uploadInfo) -> error\n44\t//     Cancels the multipart upload.\n45\t//\n46\t//   helper.CanaryUpload(ctx, key, data) -> error\n47\t//     Performs a simple canary test of the cost)\\n114\\t\\tinfo, err := b.Head(ctx, bucket, key)\\n115\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n116\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n117\\t\\t}\\n118\\t\\n119\\t\\t// Fetch body via Cloudflare for free egress\\n120\\t\\tbody, err := b.GetRange(ctx, bucket, key, 0, info.Size)\\n121\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n122\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n123\\t\\t}\\n124\\t\\n125\\t\\treturn body, info, nil\\n126\\t}\\n127\\t\\n128\\t// GetRange retrieves a byte range from an object via Cloudflare.\\n129\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetRange(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, offset, length int64) (io.ReadCloser, error) {\\n130\\t\\tbody, _, err := b.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, bucket, key, offset, length)\\n131\\t\\treturn body, err\\n132\\t}\\n133\\t\\n134\\t// GetRangeWithHeaders retrieves a byte range from an object via Cloudflare along with response headers.\\n135\\t// Falls back to direct S3 API when cfDomain is empty (e.g. CF CDN unavailable).\\n136\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, offset, length int64) (io.ReadCloser, map[string]string, error) {\\n137\\t\\tif b.cfDomain == \\\"\\\" {\\n138\\t\\t\\t// No CF domain configured — fall back to direct S3 range request.\\n139\\t\\t\\trangeHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"bytes=%d-%d\\\", offset, offset+length-1)\\n140\\t\\t\\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{\\n141\\t\\t\\t\\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\\n142\\t\\t\\t\\tKey:    aws.String(key),\\n143\\t\\t\\t\\tRange:  aws.String(rangeHeader),\\n144\\t\\t\\t})\\n145\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n146\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"direct S3 range request failed: %w\\\", err)\\n147\\t\\t\\t}\\n148\\t\\t\\treturn resp.Body, nil, nil\\n149\\t\\t}\\n150\\t\\n151\\t\\t// Construct Cloudflare download URL\\n152\\t\\tcfURL := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"https://%s/file/%s/%s\\\", b.cfDomain, bucket, url.PathEscape(key))\\n153\\t\\n154\\t\\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, \\\"GET\\\", cfURL, nil)\\n155\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n156\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create request: %w\\\", err)\\n157\\t\\t}\\n158\\t\\n159\\t\\t// Set Range header\\n160\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"bytes=%d-%d\\\", offset, offset+length-1))\\n161\\t\\n162\\t\\tresp, err := b.httpClient.Do(req)\\n163\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n164\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cloudflare request failed: %w\\\", err)\\n165\\t\\t}\\n166\\t\\n167\\t\\tif resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusPartialContent {\\n168\\t\\t\\tresp.Body.Close()\\n169\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cloudflare returned status %d\\\", resp.StatusCode)\\n170\\t\\t}\\n171\\t\\n172\\t\\t// Extract relevant headers\\n173\\t\\theaders := make(map[string]string)\\n174\\t\\tif cfStatus := resp.Header.Get(\\\"CF-Cache-Status\\\"); cfStatus != \\\"\\\" {\\n175\\t\\t\\theaders[\\\"CF-Cache-Status\\\"] = cfStatus\\n176\\t\\t}\\n177\\t\\tif cfRay := resp.Header.Get(\\\"CF-Ray\\\"); cfRay != \\\"\\\" {\\n178\\t\\t\\theaders[\\\"CF-Ray\\\"] = cfRay\\n179\\t\\t}\\n180\\t\\tif age := resp.Header.Get(\\\"Age\\\"); age != \\\"\\\" {\\n181\\t\\t\\theaders[\\\"Age\\\"] = age\\n182\\t\\t}\\n183\\t\\n184\\t\\treturn resp.Body, headers, nil\\n185\\t}\\n186\\t\\n187\\t// Head retrieves object metadata without the body.\\n188\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Head(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\\n189\\t\\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\\n190\\t\\t\\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\\n191\\t\\t\\tKey:    aws.String(key),\\n192\\t\\t})\\n193\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n194\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"HeadObject failed: %w\\\", err)\\n195\\t\\t}\\n196\\t\\n197\\t\\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\\n198\\t\\t\\tKey:          key,\\n199\\t\\t\\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\\n200\\t\\t\\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\\n201\\t\\t\\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\\n202\\t\\t\\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\\n203\\t\\t\\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\\n204\\t\\t}\\n205\\t\\n206\\t\\t// Check if this is an ARMOR-encrypted object\\n207\\t\\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\\n208\\t\\t\\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\\n209\\t\\t\\t// Use plaintext size from metadata\\n210\\t\\t\\tif am, _ := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\\n211\\t\\t\\t\\tinfo.Size = am.PlaintextSize\\n212\\t\\t\\t}\\n213\\t\\t}\\n214\\t\\n215\\t\\treturn info, nil\\n216\\t}\\n217\\t\\n218\\t// HeadVersion retrieves object metadata for a specific version.\\n219\\t// Returns ARMOR metadata (plaintext size) for the specific version.\\n220\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadVersion(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, versionID string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\\n221\\t\\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\\n222\\t\\t\\tBucket:    aws.String(bucket),\\n223\\t\\t\\tKey: aws.String(bucket),\\n255\\t\\t\\tKey:    aws.String(key),\\n256\\t\\t})\\n257\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n258\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"DeleteObject failed: %w\\\", err)\\n259\\t\\t}\\n260\\t\\treturn {\\n265\\t\\tobjects := make([]types.ObjectIdentifier, len(keys))\\n266\\t\\tfor i, key := range keys {\\n267\\t\\t\\tobjects[i] = types.ObjectIdentifier{Key: aws.String(key)}\\n268\\t\\t}\\n269\\t\\n270\\t\\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObjects(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectsInput{\\n271\\t\\t\\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\\n272\\t\\t\\tDelete: err)\\n278\\t\\t}\\n279\\t\\treturn nil\\n280\\t}\\n281\\t\\n282\\t// List objects in a bucket with optional prefix.\\n283\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\\n284\\t\\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\\n285\\t\\t\\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\\n286\\t\\t\\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\\n287\\t\\t\\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\\n288\\t\\t}\\n289\\t\\tif delimiter != \\\"\\\" {\\n290\\t\\t\\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\\n291\\t\\t}\\n292\\t\\tif maxKeys > 0 {\\n293\\t\\t\\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\\n294\\t\\t}\\n295\\t\\n296\\t\\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\\n297\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n298\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\\\", err)\\n299\\t\\t}\\n300\\t\\n301\\t\\tresult := &ListResult{\\n302\\t\\t\\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\\n303\\t\\t\\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\\n304\\t\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t\\t// Process objects\\n307\\t\\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\\n308\\t\\t\\t// Filter out .armor/ internal objects\\n309\\t\\t\\tif strings.HasPrefix(aws.ToString(obj.Key), \\\".armor/\\\") {\\n310\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n311\\t\\t\\t}\\n312\\t\\n313\\t\\t\\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\\n314\\t\\t\\t\\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\\n315\\t\\t\\t\\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\\n316\\t\\t\\t\\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\\n317\\t\\t\\t\\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\\n318\\t\\t\\t}\\n319\\t\\n320\\t\\t\\t// Try to get ARMOR metadata (requires additional HeadObject call)\\n321\\t\\t\\t// For efficiency in listings, we rely on the plaintext size being\\n322\\t\\t\\t// stored in the object's metadata which requires HeadObject per item.\\n323\\t\\t\\t// For now, we return the raw size; callers needing plaintext size\\n324\\t\\t\\t// should use HeadObject individually.\\n325\\t\\n326\\t\\t\\tresult.Objects = methods.\\n537\\ttype ReaderAtCloser struct {\\n538\\t\\tdata []byte\\n539\\t}\\n540\\t\\n541\\t// NewReaderAtCloser creates a ReaderAtCloser from a byte slice.\\n542\\tfunc NewReaderAtCloser(data []byte) *ReaderAtCloser {\\n543\\t\\treturn &ReaderAtCloser{data: data}\\n544\\t}\\n545\\t\\n546\\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\\n547\\t\\tif len(r.data) == 0 {\\n548\\t\\t\\treturn 0, io.EOF\\n549\\t\\t}\\n550\\t\\tn = copy(p, r.data)\\n551\\t\\tr.data = r.data[n:]\\n552\\t\\treturn n, nil\\n553\\t}\\n554\\t\\n555\\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) ReadAt(p {\\n561\\t\\t\\terr = io.EOF\\n562\\t\\t}\\n563\\t\\treturn n, err\\n564\\t}\\n565\\t\\n566\\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Close() error {\\n567\\t\\treturn nil\\n568\\t}\\n569\\t\\n570\\tfunc (r Package backend provides low-level multipart upload operations for S3-compatible storage.\\n2\\t//\\n3\\t// Multipart Upload Primitives\\n4\\t//\\n5\\t// This package provides low-level multipart upload operations that can be used by\\n6\\t// canary monitors and other testing tools to exercise the full multipart upload code path.\\n7\\t//\\n8\\t// The core multipart upload operations are:\\n9\\t//\\n10\\t//   CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, metadata) -> uploadID\\n11\\t//     Initiates a multipart upload and returns an upload ID that must be used for\\n12\\t//     subsequent UploadPart and CompleteMultipartUpload calls.\\n13\\t//\\n14\\t//   UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, partNumber, body, size) -> partETag\\n15\\t//     Uploads a single part to a multipart upload. Parts must be at least 5MB\\n16\\t//     (except the last part) and part numbers must be >= 1.\\n17\\t//\\n18\\t//   CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts) -> finalETag\\n19\\t//     Completes a multipart upload by combining all uploaded parts. Parts must\\n20\\t//     be sorted by part number and include all uploaded parts with their ETags.\\n21\\t//\\n22\\t//   AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\\n23\\t//     Cancels a multipart upload and releases all storage associated with it.\\n24\\t//     Should be called for cleanup when an upload fails.\\n25\\t//\\n26\\t// Helper Functions\\n27\\t//\\n28\\t// The MultipartUploadHelper provides higher-level helper functions for common\\n29\\t// multipart upload scenarios:\\n30\\t//\\n31\\t//   NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend, bucket) -> helper\\n32\\t//     Creates a helper for a specific bucket.\\n33\\t//\\n34\\t//   helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, metadata) -> uploadInfo\\n35\\t//     Creates a new multipart upload.\\n36\\t//\\n37\\t//   helper.UploadPart(ctx, uploadInfo, partNumber, data) -> error\\n38\\t//     Uploads a single part (includes validation).\\n39\\t//\\n40\\t//   helper.CompleteUpload(ctx, uploadInfo) -> finalETag\\n41\\t//     Completes the multipart upload.\\n42\\t//\\n43\\t//   helper.AbortUpload(ctx, uploadInfo) -> error\\n44\\t//     Cancels the multipart upload.\\n45\\t//\\n46\\t// *B2Backend) Delete(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) error {\\n253\\t\\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObject(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectInput{\\n254\\t\\t\\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\\n255\\t\\t\\tKey:    aws.String(key),\\n256\\t\\t})\\n257\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n258\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"DeleteObject failed: %w\\\", err)\\n259\\t\\t}\\n260\\t\\treturn nil\\n261\\t}\\n262\\t\\n263\\t// DeleteObjects removes multiple objects from B2.\\n264\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteObjects(ctx context.Context, bucket string, keys []string) error {\\n265\\t\\tobjects := make([]types.ObjectIdentifier, len(keys))\\n266\\t\\tfor i, key := range keys {\\n267\\t\\t\\tobjects[i] = types.ObjectIdentifier{Key: aws.String(key)}\\n268\\t\\t}\\n269\\t\\n270\\t\\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObjects(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectsInput{\\n271\\t\\t\\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\\n272\\t\\t\\tDelete: err)\\n278\\t\\t}\\n279\\t\\treturn nil\\n280\\t}\\n281\\t\\n282\\t// List objects in a bucket with optional prefix.\\n283\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\\n284\\t\\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\\n285\\t\\t\\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\\n286\\t\\t\\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\\n287\\t\\t\\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\\n288\\t\\t}\\n289\\t\\tif delimiter != \\\"\\\" {\\n290\\t\\t\\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\\n291\\t\\t}\\n292\\t\\tif maxKeys > 0 {\\n293\\t\\t\\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\\n294\\t\\t}\\n295\\t\\n296\\t\\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\\n297\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n298\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\\\", err)\\n299\\t\\t}\\n300\\t\\n301\\t\\tresult := &ListResult{\\n302\\t\\t\\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\\n303\\t\\t\\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\\n304\\t\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t\\t// Process objects\\n307\\t\\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\\n308\\t\\t\\t// Filter out .armor/ internal objects\\n309\\t\\t\\tif strings.HasPrefix(aws.ToString(obj.Key), \\\".armor/\\\") {\\n310\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n311\\t\\t\\t}\\n312\\t\\n313\\t\\t\\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\\n314\\t\\t\\t\\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\\n315\\t\\t\\t\\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\\n316\\t\\t\\t\\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\\n317\\t\\t\\t\\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\\n318\\t\\t\\t}\\n319\\t\\n320\\t\\t\\t// Try to get ARMOR metadata (requires additional HeadObject call)\\n321\\t\\t\\t// For efficiency in listings, we rely on the plaintext size being\\n322\\t\\t\\t// stored in the object's metadata which requires HeadObject per item.\\n323\\t\\t\\t// For now, we return the raw size; callers needing plaintext size\\n324\\t\\t\\t// should use HeadObject individually.\\n325\\t\\n326\\t\\t\\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\\n327\\t\\t}\\n328\\t\\n329\\t\\t// Process common prefixes\\n330\\t\\tfor _, prefix := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\\n331\\t\\t\\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(prefix.Prefix))\\n332\\t\\t}\\n333\\t\\n334\\t\\treturn result, nil\\n335\\t}\\n336\\t\\n337\\t// Copy copies an object in B2, supporting cross-bucket copy.\\n338\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Copy(ctx context.Context, srcBucket, srcKey, dstBucket, dstKey string, meta map[string]string, replaceMetadata bool) error {\\n339\\t\\tinput := &s3.CopyObjectInput{\\n340\\t\\t\\tBucket:     aws.String(dstBucket),\\n341\\t\\t\\tCopySource: aws.String(srcBucket + \\\"/\\\" + srcKey),\\n342\\t\\t\\tKey:        aws.String(dstKey),\\n343\\t\\t}\\n344\\t\\n345\\t\\tif replaceMetadata {\\n346\\t\\t\\tinput.MetadataDirective = types.MetadataDirectiveReplace\\n347\\t\\t\\tinput.Metadata = err)\\n353\\t\\t}\\n354\\t\\treturn nil\\n355\\t}\\n356\\t\\n357\\t// GetDirect retrieves an object directly from B2 (not via Cloudflare).\\n358\\t// Used for metadata operations where we don't need the body.\\n359\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetDirect(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\\n360\\t\\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, resp.Body, info, nil\\n382\\t}\\n383\\t\\n384\\t// ComputeETag computes an MD5 ETag for the given data.\\n385\\tfunc ComputeETag(data []byte) string {\\n386\\t\\thash := md5.Sum(data)\\n387\\t\\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\\n388\\t}\\n389\\t\\n390\\t// ComputeETagReader computes an MD5 ETag from a reader.\\n391\\tfunc ComputeETagReader(r io.Reader) (string, {\\n402\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n403\\t\\t}\\n404\\t\\tresult := make(map[string]string)\\n405\\t\\tfor k, v := range meta {\\n406\\t\\t\\tresult[k] = v\\n407\\t\\t}\\n408\\t\\treturn {\\n414\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n415\\t\\t}\\n416\\t\\tresult := make(map[string]string)\\n417\\t\\tfor k, v := range meta {\\n418\\t\\t\\tresult[k] = v\\n419\\t\\t}\\n420\\t\\treturn ||\\n479\\t\\t\\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \\\"503\\\") ||\\n480\\t\\t\\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \\\"502\\\") ||\\n481\\t\\t\\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \\\"timeout\\\") methods.\\n537\\ttype ReaderAtCloser struct {\\n538\\t\\tdata []byte\\n539\\t}\\n540\\t\\n541\\t// NewReaderAtCloser creates a ReaderAtCloser from a byte slice.\\n542\\tfunc NewReaderAtCloser(data []byte) *ReaderAtCloser {\\n543\\t\\treturn &ReaderAtCloser{data: data}\\n544\\t}\\n545\\t\\n546\\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\\n547\\t\\tif len(r.data) == 0 {\\n548\\t\\t\\treturn 0, io.EOF\\n549\\t\\t}\\n550\\t\\tn = copy(p, r.data)\\n551\\t\\tr.data = r.data[n:]\\n552\\t\\treturn n, nil\\n553\\t}\\n554\\t\\n555\\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) ReadAt(p xmlns=\\\"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\\\">%s</LifecycleConfiguration>`,\\n721\\t\\t\\tstrings.Join(rules, \\\"\\\"))\\n722\\t\\treturn []byte(xmlBody), nil\\n723\\t}\\n724\\t\\n725\\t// buildLifecycleRuleXML builds XML for a single lifecycle rule.\\n726\\tfunc (b *B2Backend) buildLifecycleRuleXML(rule types.LifecycleRule) string {\\n727\\t\\tvar parts []string\\n728\\t\\n729\\t\\t// ID\\n730\\t\\tif rule.ID != nil {\\n731\\t\\t\\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"<ID>%s</ID>\\\", *rule.ID))\\n732\\t\\t}\\n733\\t\\n734\\t\\t// Status\\n735\\t\\tif rule.Status != \\\"\\\" {\\n736\\t\\t\\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"<Status>%s</Status>\\\", rule.Status))\\n737\\t\\t}\\n738\\t\\n739\\t\\t// Filter\\n740\\t\\tif rule.Filter != nil {\\n741\\t\\t\\tfilterXML := Creates a helper for a specific bucket.\\n33\\t//\\n34\\t//   helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, metadata) -> uploadInfo\\n35\\t//     Creates a new multipart upload.\\n36\\t//\\n37\\t// to complete multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn etag, nil\n}\n\n// AbortUpload cancels the multipart upload and cleans up any uploaded parts.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) AbortUpload(ctx multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// UploadPartsInSeries uploads multiple parts in series (one after another).\n// This is simpler than parallel uploads but slower for large files.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) UploadPartsInSeries(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo, partSize int, data []byte) error {\n\tif len(data) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no data to upload\")\n\t}\n\n\tpartNumber := 1\n\toffset := 0\n\n\tfor offset < len(data) {\n\t\tend := offset + partSize\n\t\tif end > len(data) {\n\t\t\tend = len(data)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartData := data[offset:end]\n\t\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, h.bucket, info.Key, info.UploadID, int32(partNumber), io.NopCloser(nil), int64(len(partData)))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload part %d: %w\", partNumber, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tinfo.Parts = append(info.Parts, CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(partNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:       etag,\n\t\t})\n\n\t\toffset = end\n\t\tpartNumber++\n\t}\n\n\tinfo.Size = int64(len(data))\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CanaryUpload performs a simple canary test of the multipart upload system.\n// It uploads a small amount of data using multipart and verifies the result.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) CanaryUpload(ctx context.Context, key string, data []byte) error {\n\t// Create upload\n\tinfo, err := h.CreateUpload(ctx, key, map[string]string{\n\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\t\"canary-test\": \"true\",\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary create failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Upload as a single part (simplest case for canary)\n\terr = h.UploadPartsInSeries(ctx, info, len(data), data)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Try to clean up\n\t\t_ = h.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary upload failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Complete upload\n\t_, err = h.CompleteUpload(ctx, info)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Try to clean up\n\t\t_ = h.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary complete failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify the object exists\n\t_, objInfo, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, h.bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif objInfo.Size != int64(len(data)) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary verification failed: size mismatch (got %d, expected %d)\", objInfo.Size, len(data))\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up\n\terr = h.backend.Delete(ctx, h.bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary cleanup failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// VerifyMultipartUpload uploads data via multipart and downloads it back to verify integrity.\n// This is useful for canary monitors to verify the full multipart upload code path.\nfunc VerifyMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, backend Backend, bucket, key string, data []byte, partSize int) error {\n\tif len(data) < 5*1024*1024 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"data size must be at least 5MB for multipart upload\")\n\t}\n\n\tif partSize < 5*1024*1024 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"part size must be at least 5MB\")\n\t}\n\n\thelper := NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend, bucket)\n\n\t// Create multipart upload\n\tinfo, err := helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, map[string]string{\n\t\t\"Content-Type\":      \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\t\"x-amz-meta-test\":   \"multipart-verification\",\n\t\t\"x-amz-meta-size\":   fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", len(data)),\n\t\t\"x-amz-meta-source\": \"canary-monitor\",\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"create failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Upload all parts\n\terr = helper.UploadPartsInSeries(ctx, info, partSize, data)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = helper.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"upload parts failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Complete upload\n\t_, err = helper.CompleteUpload(ctx, info)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = helper.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"complete failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Download and verify\n\tbody, objInfo, err := backend.Get(ctx, bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"get failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer body.Close()\n\n\t// Verify size matches before reading the full body\n\tif objInfo.Size != int64(len(data)) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"size mismatch: got %d bytes, expected %d\", objInfo.Size, len(data))\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedData, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"read download failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(downloadedData) != len(data) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"size mismatch: downloaded %d bytes, expected %d\", len(downloadedData), len(data))\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up\n\terr = backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cleanup failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// MultipartUploadConstants defines important constants for multipart uploads.\ntype MultipartUploadConstants struct {\n\t// Minimum part size for multipart uploads (5MB)\n\tMinPartSize int64\n\n\t// Maximum part size for multipart uploads (5GB)\n\tMaxPartSize int64\n\n\t// Maximum number of parts per upload\n\tMaxPartsCount int\n\n\t// Minimum object size that should use multipart upload (5MB)\n\tMinMultipartObjectSize int64\n\n\t// Maximum object size achievable via multipart upload (5TB)\n\tMaxObjectSize int64\n}\n\n// GetMultipartUploadConstants returns the standard S3 multipart upload constants.\nfunc GetMultipartUploadConstants() MultipartUploadConstants {\n\tconst (\n\t\tminPartSize            = 5 * 1024 * 1024            // 5MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize            = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024     // 5GB\n\t\tmaxPartsCount          = 10000\n\t\tminMultipartObjectSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024            // 5MB\n\t\tmaxObjectSize          = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\n\t)\n\n\treturn MultipartUploadConstants{\n\t\tMinPartSize:            minPartSize,\n\t\tMaxPartSize:            maxPartSize,\n\t\tMaxPartsCount:         maxPartsCount,\n\t\tMinMultipartObjectSize: minMultipartObjectSize,\n\t\tMaxObjectSize:          maxObjectSize,\n\t}\n}\n\n// CalculateOptimalPartSize calculates the optimal part size for a given file size.\n// It aims to use parts between 10MB and 100MB for efficiency while staying within limits.\nfunc CalculateOptimalPartSize(fileSize int64) int64 {\n\tconst (\n\t\tminPartSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024  // 10MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize = 100 * 1024 * 1024 // 100MB\n\t\tmaxParts    = 10000\n\t)\n\n\t// For small files, use the minimum part size\n\tif fileSize <= minPartSize*maxParts {\n\t\treturn minPartSize\n\t}\n\n\t// For larger files, calculate a part size that keeps us under maxParts\n\tpartSize := fileSize / maxParts\n\tif partSize < minPartSize {\n\t\tpartSize = minPartSize\n\t}\n\tif partSize > maxPartSize {\n\t\tpartSize = maxPartSize\n\t}\n\n\treturn partSize\n}\n","numLines":309,"startLine":1,"totalLines":309}}}

aws.String(bucket),\n76\t\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n77\t\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n78\t\t\t\tMultipartUpload: &types.CompletedMultipartUpload{\n79\t\t\t\t\tParts: awsParts,\n80\t\t\t\t},\n81\t\t\t})\n82\t\t\tif err != nil {\n83\t\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n84\t\t\t}\n85\t\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n86\t\t}\n87\t\treturn t.B2Backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\tfunc (t *testableB2Backend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n91\t\tif t.mockS3Client != nil {\n92\t\t\t_, err := t.mockS3Client.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput{\n93\t\t\t\tBucket: cancellation in multipart operations.\n372\tfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartContextCancellation(t *testing.T) {\n373\t\ttests := []struct {\n374\t\t\tname      string\n375\t\t\toperation string\n376\t\t}{\n377\t\t\t{\"CreateMultipartUpload cancellation\", \"create\"},\n378\t\t\t{\"UploadPart cancellation\", \"upload\"},\n379\t\t\t{\"CompleteMultipartUpload cancellation\", \"complete\"},\n380\t\t\t{\"AbortMultipartUpload cancellation\", \"abort\"},\n381\t\t}\n382\t\n383\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n384\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n385\t\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n386\t\t\t\tcancel() // Cancel before calling the operation\n387\t\n388\t\t\t\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n389\t\t\t\t\t// Mock functions that check for canceled context\n390\t\t\t\t\tcreateMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n391\t\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n392\t\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n393\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n394\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n395\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput{UploadId: aws.String(\"test-upload-id\")}, nil\n396\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n397\t\t\t\t\t},\n398\t\t\t\t\tuploadPartFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.UploadPartInput) (*s3.UploadPartOutput, error) {\n399\t\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n400\t\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n401\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n402\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n403\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.UploadPartOutput{ETag: aws.String(\"test-etag\")}, nil\n404\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n405\t\t\t\t\t},\n406\t\t\t\t\tcompleteMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n407\t\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n408\t\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n409\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n410\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n411\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput{ETag: aws.String(\"final-etag\")}, nil\n412\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n413\t\t\t\t\t},\n414\t\t\t\t\tabortMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.AbortMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n415\t\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n416\t\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n417\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n418\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n419\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.AbortMultipartUploadOutput{}, nil\n420\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n421\t\t\t\t\t},\n422\t\t\t\t}\n423\t\n424\t\t\t\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n425\t\n426\t\t\t\tvar err error\n427\t\t\t\tswitch tt.operation {\n428\t\t\t\tcase \"create\":\n429\t\t\t\t\t_, err = backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", nil)\n430\t\t\t\tcase \"upload\":\n431\t\t\t\t\t_, err = backend.UploadPart(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\", 1, nil, 100)\n432\t\t\t\tcase \"complete\":\n433\t\t\t\t\t_, err = backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\", []CompletedPart{})\n434\t\t\t\tcase \"abort\":\n435\t\t\t\t\terr = backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\")\n436\t\t\t\t}\n437\t\n438\t\t\t\t// Note: With the current mock implementation, context cancellation is checked\n439\t\t\t\t// inside the mock functions. In real scenarios, the AWS SDK handles this.\n440\t\t\t\t// This test verifies that our test infrastructure properly handles context.\n441\t\t\t\tif err != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\n442\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Got expected context error: %v\", err)\n443\t\t\t\t}\n444\t\t\t})\n445\t\t}\n446\t}\n447\t\n448\t// TestB2Backend_MultipartThresholds documents multipart upload thresholds and limits.\n449\tfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartThresholds(t *testing.T) {\n450\t\t// S3 Multipart Upload Thresholds and Limits:\n451\t\t//\n452\t\t// Minimum object size for multipart upload:\n453\t\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes) - recommended threshold\n454\t\t//   - Objects smaller than this should use single-part PutObject\n455\t\t//\n456\t\t// Minimum part size (except last part):\n457\t\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes)\n458\t\t//   - All parts except the last must be at least this size\n459\t\t//\n460\t\t// Maximum part size:\n461\t\t//   - 5GB (5,368,709,120 bytes)\n462\t\t//\n463\t\t// Maximum number of parts:\n464\t\t//   - 10,000 parts per upload\n465\t\t//\n466\t\t// Maximum object size via multipart upload:\n467\t\t//   - 5TB (5,497,558,138,880 bytes)\n468\t\t//\n469\t\t// Recommended part size for large files:\n470\t\t//   - 10MB to 100MB is typical\n471\t\t//   - Larger parts = fewer API calls = faster uploads\n472\t\t//   - Smaller parts = better recovery from failures = more retryable\n473\t\t//\n474\t\t// For ARMOR:\n475\t\t//   - Part sizes should be multiples of the encryption block size (4096 bytes)\n476\t\t//   - This ensures proper alignment for encryption operations\n477\t\t//   - Typical ARMOR part size: 5MB, 10MB, or 50MB (all multiples of 4096)\n478\t\n479\t\tconst (\n480\t\t\tminMultipartObjectSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\n481\t\t\tminPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\n482\t\t\tmaxPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5GB\n483\t\t\tmaxPartsCount         = 10000\n484\t\t\tmaxObjectSize         = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\n485\t\t\tarmorBlockSize        = 4096\n486\t\t)\n487\t\n488\t\tif minMultipartObjectSize < minPartSize {\n489\t\t\tt.Error(\"Minimum multipart object size should be at least minimum part size\")\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\tif maxPartSize*maxPartsCount < maxObjectSize {\n493\t\t\tt.Error(\"Maximum object size should be achievable with max parts and max part size\")\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\t// Verify common part sizes are multiples of ARMOR block size\n497\t\tcommonPartSizes := []int64{\n498\t\t\t5 * 1024 * 1024,   // 5MB\n499\t\t\t10 * 1024 * 1024,  // 10MB\n500\t\t\t50 * 1024 * 1024,  // 50MB\n501\t\t\t100 * 1024 * 1024, // 100MB\n502\t\t}\n503\t\n504\t\tfor _, size := range commonPartSizes {\n505\t\t\tif size%int64(armorBlockSize) != 0 {\n506\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Part size %d is not a multiple of ARMOR block size %d\", size, armorBlockSize)\n507\t\t\t}\n508\t\t}\n509\t}\n510\t\n511\t// TestB2Backend_MultipartUploadWorkflow tests the complete multipart upload workflow.\n512\tfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartUploadWorkflow(t *testing.T) {\n513\t\tctx := context.Background()\n514\t\n515\t\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n516\t\t\tcreateMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n517\t\t\t\treturn &s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput{\n518\t\t\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(\"workflow-upload-id\"),\n519\t\t\t\t}, nil\n520\t\t\t},\n521\t\t\tuploadPartFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input \"application/octet-stream\",\n540\t\t})\n541\t\tif err != nil {\n542\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %v\", err)\n543\t\t}\n544\t\tif uploadID != \"workflow-upload-id\" {\n545\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected upload ID 'workflow-upload-id', got '%s'\", uploadID)\n546\t\t}\n547\t\n548\t\t// Step 2: Upload parts\n549\t\tvar parts []CompletedPart\n550\t\tfor i := 1; i <= 3; i++ fmt.Errorf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.UploadId), nil\n\t}\n\treturn t.B2Backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, meta)\n}\n\nfunc (t *testableB2Backend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tif t.mockS3Client != nil {\n\t\tresp, err := t.mockS3Client.UploadPart(ctx, &s3.UploadPartInput{\n\t\t\tBucket:        aws.String(bucket),\n\t\t\tKey:           aws.String(key),\n\t\t\tUploadId:      aws.String(uploadID),\n\t\t\tPartNumber:    aws.Int32(partNumber),\n\t\t\tBody:          body,\n\t\t\tContentLength: aws.Int64(size),\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"UploadPart failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n\t}\n\treturn t.B2Backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, partNumber, body, size)\n}\n\nfunc (t *testableB2Backend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tif t.mockS3Client != nil {\n\t\tawsParts := make([]types.CompletedPart, len(parts))\n\t\tfor i, p := range parts {\n\t\t\tawsParts[i] = types.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\t\tETag:       aws.String(p.ETag),\n\t\t\t\tPartNumber: aws.Int32(p.PartNumber),\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tresp, err := t.mockS3Client.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput{\n\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n\t\t\tMultipartUpload: &types.CompletedMultipartUpload{\n\t\t\t\tParts: awsParts,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n\t}\n\treturn t.B2Backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n}\n\nfunc (t *testableB2Backend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tif t.mockS3Client != nil {\n\t\t_, err := t.mockS3Client.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput{\n\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"AbortMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn t.B2Backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n}\n\n// mockS3Client is a mock S3 client for error)\n\tuploadPartFunc           func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.UploadPartInput) (*s3.UploadPartOutput, error)\n\tcompleteMultipartUploadFunc func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput, error)\n\tabortMultipartUploadFunc  func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput) exist\"),\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpectErrorContains: \"upload\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tctx := context.Background()\n\t\t\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{}\n\n\t\t\tswitch tt.operation {\n\t\t\tcase \"create\":\n\t\t\t\tmockClient.createMultipartUploadFunc = tt.mockFunc.(func(context.Context, *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error))\n\t\t\t\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n\t\t\t\t_, err := backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error, got nil\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase \"upload\":\n\t\t\t\tmockClient.uploadPartFunc = tt.mockFunc.(func(context.Context, *s3.UploadPartInput) (*s3.UploadPartOutput, error))\n\t\t\t\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n\t\t\t\t_, err := backend.UploadPart(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\", 1, nil, 100)\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error, got nil\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error, got nil\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase \"abort\":\n\t\t\t\tmockClient.abortMultipartUploadFunc = tt.mockFunc.(func(context.Context, *s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput) \"upload-id\")\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error, got nil\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestB2Backend_MultipartContextCancellation tests context cancellation in multipart operations.\nfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartContextCancellation(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\toperation string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"CreateMultipartUpload cancellation\", \"create\"},\n\t\t{\"UploadPart cancellation\", \"upload\"},\n\t\t{\"CompleteMultipartUpload cancellation\", \"complete\"},\n\t\t{\"AbortMultipartUpload cancellation\", \"abort\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\t\t\tcancel() // Cancel before calling the operation\n\n\t\t\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n\t\t\t\t// Mock functions that check for canceled context\n\t\t\t\tcreateMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput{UploadId: aws.String(\"test-upload-id\")}, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tuploadPartFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.UploadPartInput) (*s3.UploadPartOutput, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.UploadPartOutput{ETag: aws.String(\"test-etag\")}, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tcompleteMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput{ETag: aws.String(\"final-etag\")}, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tabortMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.AbortMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ctx.Err()\n\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.AbortMultipartUploadOutput{}, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n\n\t\t\tvar err error\n\t\t\tswitch tt.operation {\n\t\t\tcase \"create\":\n\t\t\t\t_, err = backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\tcase \"upload\":\n\t\t\t\t_, err = backend.UploadPart(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\", 1, nil, 100)\n\t\t\tcase \"complete\":\n\t\t\t\t_, err = backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\", []CompletedPart{})\n\t\t\tcase \"abort\":\n\t\t\t\terr = backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Note: With the current mock implementation, context cancellation is checked\n\t\t\t// inside the mock functions. In real scenarios, the AWS SDK handles this.\n\t\t\t// This test verifies that our test infrastructure properly handles context.\n\t\t\tif err != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Got expected context error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestB2Backend_MultipartThresholds documents multipart upload thresholds and limits.\nfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartThresholds(t *testing.T) {\n\t// S3 Multipart Upload Thresholds and Limits:\n\t//\n\t// Minimum object size for multipart upload:\n\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes) - recommended threshold\n\t//   - Objects smaller than this should use single-part PutObject\n\t//\n\t// Minimum part size (except last part):\n\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes)\n\t//   - All parts except the last must be at least this size\n\t//\n\t// Maximum part size:\n\t//   - 5GB (5,368,709,120 bytes)\n\t//\n\t// Maximum number of parts:\n\t//   - 10,000 parts per upload\n\t//\n\t// Maximum object size via multipart upload:\n\t//   - 5TB (5,497,558,138,880 bytes)\n\t//\n\t// Recommended part size for large files:\n\t//   - 10MB to 100MB is typical\n\t//   - Larger parts = fewer API calls = faster uploads\n\t//   - Smaller parts = better recovery from failures = more retryable\n\t//\n\t// For ARMOR:\n\t//   - Part sizes should be multiples of the encryption block size (4096 bytes)\n\t//   - This ensures proper alignment for encryption operations\n\t//   - Typical ARMOR part size: 5MB, 10MB, or 50MB (all multiples of 4096)\n\n\tconst (\n\t\tminMultipartObjectSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\n\t\tminPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5GB\n\t\tmaxPartsCount         = 10000\n\t\tmaxObjectSize         = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\n\t\tarmorBlockSize        = 4096\n\t)\n\n\tif minMultipartObjectSize < minPartSize {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Minimum multipart object size should be at least minimum part size\")\n\t}\n\n\tif maxPartSize*maxPartsCount < maxObjectSize {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Maximum object size should be achievable with max parts and max part size\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify common part sizes are multiples of ARMOR block size\n\tcommonPartSizes := []int64{\n\t\t5 * 1024 * 1024,   // 5MB\n\t\t10 * 1024 * 1024,  // 10MB\n\t\t50 * 1024 * 1024,  // 50MB\n\t\t100 * 1024 * 1024, // 100MB\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, size := range commonPartSizes {\n\t\tif size%int64(armorBlockSize) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Part size %d is not a multiple of ARMOR block size %d\", size, armorBlockSize)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestB2Backend_MultipartUploadWorkflow tests the complete multipart upload workflow.\nfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartUploadWorkflow(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n\t\tcreateMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\treturn &s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput{\n\t\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(\"workflow-upload-id\"),\n\t\t\t}, nil\n\t\t},\n\t\tuploadPartFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.UploadPartInput) (*s3.UploadPartOutput, error) {\n\t\t\tpartNum := aws.ToInt32(input.PartNumber)\n\t\t\treturn &s3.UploadPartOutput{\n\t\t\t\tETag: aws.String(fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-part-%d\", partNum)),\n\t\t\t}, nil\n\t\t},\n\t\tcompleteMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\treturn &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadOutput{\n\t\t\t\tETag: aws.String(\"final-workflow-etag\"),\n\t\t\t\tLocation: aws.String(\"https://test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/test-key\"),\n\t\t\t}, nil\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n\n\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\n\tuploadID, err := backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", map[string]string{\n\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif uploadID != \"workflow-upload-id\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected upload ID 'workflow-upload-id', got '%s'\", uploadID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Upload parts\n\tvar parts []CompletedPart\n\tfor i := 1; i <= 3; i++ {\n\t\tetag, err := backend.UploadPart(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", uploadID, int32(i), nil, 5*1024*1024)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"UploadPart %d failed: %v\", i, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tparts = append(parts, CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(i),\n\t\t\tETag:        etag,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Complete upload\n\tfinalETag, err := backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", uploadID, parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif finalETag != \"final-workflow-etag\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected final ETag 'final-workflow-etag', got '%s'\", finalETag)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"✓ Complete multipart workflow test passed\")\n}\n\n// TestB2Backend_AbortMultipartUploadWorkflow tests aborting a multipart upload.\nfunc TestB2Backend_AbortMultipartUploadWorkflow(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n\t\tcreateMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\treturn &s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput{\n\t\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(\"abort-test-upload-id\"),\n\t\t\t}, nil\n\t\t},\n\t\tabortMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.AbortMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\tif aws.ToString(input.UploadId) != \"abort-test-upload-id\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected upload ID: %s\", aws.ToString(input.UploadId))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn &s3.AbortMultipartUploadOutput{}, nil\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n\n\t// Create upload\n\tuploadID, err := backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Abort multipart upload test passed\")\n}\n\n// TestB2Backend_MultipartMetadataHandling tests metadata handling in multipart uploads.\nfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartMetadataHandling(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := \"custom-value\",\n\t}\n\n\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n\t\tcreateMultipartUploadFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput) (*s3.CreateMultipartUploadOutput, error) {\n\t\t\t// Verify metadata is passed correctly\n\t\t\tif input.Metadata != nil {\n\t\t\t\tfor k, v := range testMetadata {\n\t\t\t\t\tif val, ok := input.Metadata[k]; !ok || val != v
