TestB2Backend_MultipartPartNumberValidation(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname       string\n\t\tpartNumber int32\n\t\tshouldFail bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"Valid part number 1\", 1, false},\n\t\t{\"Valid part number 5000\", 5000, false},\n\t\t{\"Valid part number 10000\", 10000, false},\n\t\t{\"Invalid part number 0\", 0, true},\n\t\t{\"Invalid part number -1\", -1, true},\n\t\t{\"Invalid part number 10001\", 10001, true},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tmockClient := &mockS3Client{\n\t\t\t\tuploadPartFunc: func(ctx context.Context, input *s3.UploadPartInput) (*s3.UploadPartOutput, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tpartNum := aws.ToInt32(input.PartNumber)\n\t\t\t\t\tif partNum < 1 || partNum > 10000 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidPartNumber: Part number must be between 1 and 10000\")\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn &s3.UploadPartOutput{ETag: aws.String(\"test-etag\")}, nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tbackend := &testableB2Backend{mockS3Client: mockClient}\n\n\t\t\t_, err := backend.UploadPart(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", \"upload-id\", tt.partNumber, nil, 5*1024*1024)\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldFail && err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error for invalid part number, got nil\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !tt.shouldFail && err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected success for valid part number, got error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// mockB2BackendForTesting creates a mock backend for testing without requiring real B2 credentials.\n// This is useful for canary monitors and other testing scenarios.\ntype mockB2BackendForTesting struct {\n\tuploadIDCounter int\n\tuploads         map[string]*testUploadState\n\tparts           map[string][]testPart\n\tobjects         map[string][]byte\n}\n\ntype testUploadState struct {\n\tuploadID  string\n\tbucket    string\n\tkey       string\n\tmetadata  map[string]string\n\tcreatedAt time.Time\n}\n\ntype testPart struct {\n\tpartNumber int32\n\tdata       []byte\n\tetag       string\n}\n\n// NewMockB2BackendForTesting creates a new mock backend for testing multipart operations.\n// This backend simulates S3 multipart upload behavior without requiring real credentials.\nfunc NewMockB2BackendForTesting() *mockB2BackendForTesting {\n\treturn &mockB2BackendForTesting{\n\t\tuploads: make(map[string]*testUploadState),\n\t\tparts:   make(map[string][]testPart),\n\t\tobjects: make(map[string][]byte),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockB2BackendForTesting) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tm.uploadIDCounter++\n\tuploadID := fmt.Sprintf(\"mock-upload-%d\", m.uploadIDCounter)\n\n\tm.uploads[uploadID] = &testUploadState{\n\t\tuploadID:  uploadID,\n\t\tbucket:    bucket,\n\t\tkey:       key,\n\t\tmetadata:  meta,\n\t\tcreatedAt: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\tm.parts[uploadID] = []testPart{}\n\n\treturn uploadID, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockB2BackendForTesting) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tstate, ok := m.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"NoSuchUpload: The specified multipart upload does not exist\")\n\t}\n\n\tif state.bucket != bucket || state.key != key {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidRequest: Bucket/key mismatch\")\n\t}\n\n\tif partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidPartNumber: Part number must be between 1 and 10000\")\n\t}\n\n\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read part data: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif size > 0 && int64(len(data)) != size {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"DataLengthMismatch: expected %d bytes, got %d\", size, len(data))\n\t}\n\n\tetag := fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%d-%x\", partNumber, len(data))\n\n\tm.parts[uploadID] = append(m.parts[uploadID], testPart{\n\t\tpartNumber: partNumber,\n\t\tdata:       data,\n\t\tetag:       etag,\n\t})\n\n\treturn etag, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockB2BackendForTesting) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\tstate, ok := m.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"NoSuchUpload: The specified multipart upload does not exist\")\n\t}\n\n\tif state.bucket != bucket || state.key != key {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidRequest: Bucket/key mismatch\")\n\t}\n\n\tuploadedParts := m.parts[uploadID]\n\tif len(parts) != len(uploadedParts) {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidPart: Mismatch in part count\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort parts by part number\n\tpartMap := make(map[int32]*testPart)\n\tfor i := range uploadedParts {\n\t\tpartMap[uploadedParts[i].partNumber] = &uploadedParts[i]\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all parts and combine data\n\tvar combinedData []byte\n\tfor _, p := range parts {\n\t\tpart, ok := partMap[p.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidPart: Part %d not found\", p.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif part.etag != p.ETag {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidPartETag: ETag mismatch for part %d\", p.PartNumber)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcombinedData = append(combinedData, part.data...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Store the combined object\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tm.objects[objectKey] = combinedData\n\n\t// Clean up upload state\n\tdelete(m.uploads, uploadID)\n\tdelete(m.parts, uploadID)\n\n\tfinalETag := fmt.Sprintf(\"final-%x\", len(combinedData))\n\treturn finalETag, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockB2BackendForTesting) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\tstate, ok := m.uploads[uploadID]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"NoSuchUpload: The specified multipart upload does not exist\")\n\t}\n\n\tif state.bucket != bucket || state.key != key {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"InvalidRequest: Bucket/key mismatch\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up upload state\n\tdelete(m.uploads, uploadID)\n\tdelete(m.parts, uploadID)\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *mockB2BackendForTesting) Get(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n\tdata, ok := m.objects[objectKey]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"NoSuchKey: The specified key does not exist\")\n\t}\n\n\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n\t\tKey:          key,\n\t\tSize:         int64(len(data)),\n\t\tContentType:  \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x\", len(data)),\n\t\tLastModified: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\treturn io.NopCloser(bytes.NewReader(data)), info, nil\n}\n\n// TestMockB2Backend_MultipartUploadIntegration tests the mock backend with a complete workflow.\nfunc TestMockB2Backend_MultipartUploadIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := []CompletedPart\n\tpartData := [][]byte{\n\t\tbytes.Repeat([]byte{0x00}, 5*1024*1024), // 5MB part 1\n\t\tbytes.Repeat([]byte{0x01}, 5*1024*1024), // 5MB part 2\n\t\tbytes.Repeat([]byte{0x02}, 3*1024*1024), // 3MB part 3 (irregular)\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, data := range partData {\n\t\tpartNum := int32(i + 1)\n\t\tetag, err := backend.UploadPart(ctx, \"test-bucket\", \"test-key\", uploadID, B2 then\n61\t\t\t// records checksum-typed multipart parts and rejects a\n62\t\t\t// CompleteMultipartUpload that supplies only ETags with InvalidPart.\n63\t\t\to.RequestChecksumCalculation = aws.RequestChecksumCalculationWhenRequired\n64\t\t\to.ResponseChecksumValidation = aws.ResponseChecksumValidationWhenRequired\n65\t\t})\n66\t\n67\t\treturn &B2Backend{\n68\t\t\ts3Client:   s3Client,\n69\t\t\tregion:     cfg.Region,\n70\t\t\tendpoint:   cfg.Endpoint,\n71\t\t\tcfDomain:   cfg.CFDomain,\n72\t\t\thttpClient: &http.Client{Timeout: 30 * time.Minute},\n73\t\t}, nil\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// Put stores an object in B2.\n77\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Put(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, body io.Reader, size int64, meta map[string]string) error {\n78\t\t// B2 rejects requests without a Content-Length header. With an unseekable\n79\t\t// body the Go transport falls back to Transfer-Encoding: chunked and drops\n80\t\t// Content-Length (AWS accepts that, B2 does not), so spool to a temp file\n81\t\t// to give the transport a seekable body with a known length.\n82\t\tif _, ok := body.(io.ReadSeeker); !ok {\n83\t\t\ttmp, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-put-*\")\n84\t\t\tif err != nil {\n85\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n86\t\t\t}\n87\t\t\tdefer os.Remove(tmp.Name())\n88\t\t\tdefer tmp.Close()\n89\t\t\tif _, err := io.Copy(tmp, body); err != nil {\n90\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n91\t\t\t}\n92\t\t\tif _, err := tmp.Seek(0, io.SeekStart); err != nil {\n93\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n94\t\t\t}\n95\t\t\tbody = tmp\n96\t\t}\n97\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.PutObject(ctx, &s3.PutObjectInput{\n98\t\t\tBucket:      aws.String(bucket),\n99\t\t\tKey:         aws.String(key),\n100\t\t\tBody:        body,\n101\t\t\tContentLength: aws.Int64(size),\n102\t\t\tMetadata:    toS3Metadata(meta),\n103\t\t})\n104\t\tif err != nil {\n105\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject failed: %w\", err)\n106\t\t}\n107\t\treturn nil\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t// Get retrieves an object's full content and metadata.\n111\t// For ARMOR, this uses the Cloudflare domain for free egress.\n112\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Get(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n113\t\t// First get metadata via HeadObject (direct to B2, no egress cost)\n114\t\tinfo, err := b.Head(ctx, bucket, key)\n115\t\tif err != nil {\n116\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n117\t\t}\n118\t\n119\t\t// Fetch body via Cloudflare for free egress\n120\t\tbody, err := b.GetRange(ctx, bucket, key, 0, info.Size)\n121\t\tif err != nil {\n122\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n123\t\t}\n124\t\n125\t\treturn body, info, nil\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\t// GetRange retrieves a byte headers.\n135\t// Falls back to direct S3 API when cfDomain is empty (e.g. CF CDN unavailable).\n136\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, offset, length int64) (io.ReadCloser, map[string]string, error) {\n137\t\tif b.cfDomain == \"\" {\n138\t\t\t// No CF domain configured — fall back to direct S3 range request.\n139\t\t\trangeHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", offset, offset+length-1)\n140\t\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{\n141\t\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n142\t\t\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n143\t\t\t\tRange:  aws.String(rangeHeader),\n144\t\t\t})\n145\t\t\tif err != nil {\n146\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"direct S3 range request failed: %w\", err)\n157\t\t}\n158\t\n159\t\t// Set Range header\n160\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", offset, offset+length-1))\n161\t\n162\t\tresp, err := b.httpClient.Do(req)\n163\t\tif am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n211\t\t\t\tinfo.Size = am.PlaintextSize\n212\t\t\t}\n213\t\t}\n214\t\n215\t\treturn info, nil\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// HeadVersion retrieves object metadata for a specific version.\n219\t// Returns ARMOR metadata (plaintext size) for the specific version.\n220\tfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadVersion(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, versionID string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\n221\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\n222\t\t\tBucket:    aws.String(bucket),\n223\t\t\tKey:       aws.String(key),\n224\t\t\tVersionId: objects from B2.\n264\tfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteObjects(ctx context.Context, bucket string, keys []string) error {\n265\t\tobjects := make([]types.ObjectIdentifier, len(keys))\n266\t\tfor i, key := range keys {\n267\t\t\tobjects[i] = types.ObjectIdentifier{Key: aws.String(key)}\n268\t\t}\n269\t\n270\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObjects(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectsInput{\n271\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n272\t\t\tDelete: &types.Delete{\n273\t\t\t\tObjects: objects,\n274\t\t\t},\n275\t\t})\n276\t\tif err != nil {\n277\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteObjects failed: %w\", err)\n278\t\t}\n279\t\treturn nil\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\t// List objects in a bucket with optional prefix.\n283\tfunc (b *B2Backend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\n284\t\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n285\t\t\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\n286\t\t\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\n287\t\t\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\n288\t\t}\n289\t\tif delimiter != \"\" {\n290\t\t\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\n291\t\t}\n292\t\tif maxKeys > 0 {\n293\t\t\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\n294\t\t}\n295\t\n296\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\n297\t\tif err != nil {\n298\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\", err)\n299\t\t}\n300\t\n301\t\tresult := &ListResult{\n302\t\t\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n303\t\t\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\n304\t\t}\n305\t\n306\t\t// Process objects\n307\t\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\n308\t\t\t// Filter out .armor/ internal objects\n309\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(aws.ToString(obj.Key), \".armor/\") {\n310\t\t\t\tcontinue\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\n313\t\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n314\t\t\t\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\n315\t\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\n316\t\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\n317\t\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\n318\t\t\t}\n319\t\n320\t\t\t// Try to get ARMOR metadata (requires additional HeadObject call)\n321\t\t\t// For efficiency in listings, we rely on the plaintext size being\n322\t\t\t// stored in the object's metadata which requires HeadObject per item.\n323\t\t\t// For now, we return the raw size; callers needing plaintext size\n324\t\t\t// should use HeadObject individually.\n325\t\n326\t\t\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\n327\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\t// Process common prefixes\n330\t\tfor _, prefix := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\n331\t\t\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(prefix.Prefix))\n332\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\treturn result, nil\n335\t}\n336\t\n337\t// Copy copies an object in B2, supporting cross-bucket copy.\n338\tfunc (b *B2Backend) Copy(ctx context.Context, srcBucket, srcKey, dstBucket, dstKey string, meta map[string]string, replaceMetadata bool) error {\n339\t\tinput := &s3.CopyObjectInput{\n340\t\t\tBucket:     aws.String(dstBucket),\n341\t\t\tCopySource: aws.String(srcBucket + \"/\" + srcKey),\n342\t\t\tKey:        aws.String(dstKey),\n343\t\t}\n344\t\n345\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n346\t\t\tinput.MetadataDirective = types.MetadataDirectiveReplace\n347\t\t\tinput.Metadata = toS3Metadata(meta)\n348\t\t}\n349\t\n350\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.CopyObject(ctx, input)\n351\t\tif err != nil {\n352\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"CopyObject failed: %w\", err)\n353\t\t}\n354\t\treturn nil\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// GetDirect retrieves an object directly from B2 (not via Cloudflare).\n358\t// Used for metadata operations where we don't need the body.\n359\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetDirect(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n360\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{\n361\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n362\t\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n363\t\t})\n364\t\tif err != nil {\n365\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"GetObject failed: %w\", err)\n366\t\t}\n367\t\n368\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n369\t\t\tKey:          key,\n370\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n371\t\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n372\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n373\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n374\t\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n378\t\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n379\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\treturn resp.Body, info, nil\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// ComputeETag computes an MD5 ETag for the given data.\n385\tfunc ComputeETag(data []byte) string {\n386\t\thash := md5.Sum(data)\n387\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// ComputeETagReader computes an MD5 ETag from a reader.\n391\tfunc ComputeETagReader(r io.Reader) (string, error) {\n392\t\thash := md5.New()\n393\t\tif _, err := io.Copy(hash, r); err != nil {\n394\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n395\t\t}\n396\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash.Sum(nil)), nil\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\t// toS3Metadata converts a map to S3 metadata format.\n400\tfunc toS3Metadata(meta map[string]string) map[string]string {\n401\t\tif meta == nil {\n402\t\t\treturn nil\n403\t\t}\n404\t\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n405\t\tfor k, v := range meta {\n406\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n407\t\t}\n408\t\treturn result\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t// fromS3Metadata converts S3 metadata to a regular map.\n412\tfunc fromS3Metadata(meta map[string]string) map[string]string {\n413\t\tif meta == nil {\n414\t\t\treturn nil\n415\t\t}\n416\t\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n417\t\tfor k, v := range meta {\n418\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n419\t\t}\n420\t\treturn result\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t// ListRaw lists objects without filtering .armor/ internal objects.\n424\t// Used for internal operations such as manifest discovery where all keys\n425\t// under a prefix (including .armor/ prefixed keys) must be enumerated.\n426\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListRaw(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\n427\t\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n428\t\t\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\n429\t\t\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\n430\t\t\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\n431\t\t}\n432\t\tif delimiter != \"\" {\n433\t\t\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\n434\t\t}\n435\t\tif maxKeys > 0 {\n436\t\t\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\n437\t\t}\n438\t\n439\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\n440\t\tif err != nil {\n441\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\", err)\n442\t\t}\n443\t\n444\t\tresult := &ListResult{\n445\t\t\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n446\t\t\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\n450\t\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n451\t\t\t\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\n452\t\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\n453\t\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\n454\t\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\n455\t\t\t}\n456\t\t\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\n457\t\t}\n458\t\n459\t\tfor _, p := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\n460\t\t\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(p.Prefix))\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\treturn result, nil\n464\t}\n465\t\n466\t// S3Client returns the underlying S3 client for advanced operations.\n467\tfunc (b *B2Backend) S3Client() *s3.Client {\n468\t\treturn b.s3Client\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t// IsRetryableError checks if an error is retryable.\n472\tfunc IsRetryableError(err error) bool {\n473\t\tif err == nil {\n474\t\t\treturn false\n475\t\t}\n476\t\t// Check for common retryable conditions\n477\t\terrStr := err.Error()\n478\t\treturn strings.Contains(errStr, \"5xx\") ||\n479\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"503\") ||\n480\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"502\") ||\n481\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"timeout\") ||\n482\t\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"connection reset\")\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// ListBuckets lists all buckets.\n486\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListBuckets(ctx context.Context) ([]BucketInfo, error) {\n487\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListBuckets(ctx, &s3.ListBucketsInput{})\n488\t\tif err != nil {\n489\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListBuckets failed: %w\", err)\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\tbuckets := make([]BucketInfo, len(resp.Buckets))\n493\t\tfor i, bucket := range resp.Buckets {\n494\t\t\tbuckets[i] = BucketInfo{\n495\t\t\t\tName:         aws.ToString(bucket.Name),\n496\t\t\t\tCreationDate: aws.ToTime(bucket.CreationDate),\n497\t\t\t}\n498\t\t}\n499\t\n500\t\treturn buckets, nil\n501\t}\n502\t\n503\t// CreateBucket creates a new bucket.\n504\tfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n505\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.CreateBucket(ctx, &s3.CreateBucketInput{\n506\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n507\t\t})\n508\t\tif err != nil {\n509\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"CreateBucket failed: %w\", err)\n510\t\t}\n511\t\treturn nil\n512\t}\n513\t\n514\t// DeleteBucket deletes an empty bucket.\n515\tfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n516\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteBucket(ctx, &s3.DeleteBucketInput{\n517\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n518\t\t})\n519\t\tif err != nil {\n520\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteBucket failed: %w\", err)\n521\t\t}\n522\t\treturn nil\n523\t}\n524\t\n525\t// HeadBucket checks if a bucket exists.\n526\tfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n527\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.HeadBucket(ctx, &s3.HeadBucketInput{\n528\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n529\t\t})\n530\t\tif err != nil {\n531\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"HeadBucket failed: %w\", err)\n532\t\t}\n533\t\treturn nil\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// ReaderAtCloser wraps a reader with Close and ReadAt methods.\n537\ttype ReaderAtCloser struct {\n538\t\tdata []byte\n539\t}\n540\t\n541\t// NewReaderAtCloser creates a ReaderAtCloser from a byte slice.\n542\tfunc NewReaderAtCloser(data []byte) *ReaderAtCloser {\n543\t\treturn &ReaderAtCloser{data: data}\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\n547\t\tif len(r.data) == 0 {\n548\t\t\treturn 0, io.EOF\n549\t\t}\n550\t\tn = copy(p, r.data)\n551\t\tr.data = r.data[n:]\n552\t\treturn n, nil\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) ReadAt(p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error) {\n556\t\tif off >= int64(len(r.data)) {\n557\t\t\treturn 0, io.EOF\n558\t\t}\n559\t\tn = copy(p, r.data[off:])\n560\t\tif n < len(p) {\n561\t\t\terr = io.EOF\n562\t\t}\n563\t\treturn n, err\n564\t}\n565\t\n566\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Close() error {\n567\t\treturn nil\n568\t}\n569\t\n570\tfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Bytes() []byte {\n571\t\treturn r.data\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// CreateMultipartUpload initiates a multipart upload.\n575\tfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n576\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput{\n577\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n578\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n579\t\t\tMetadata: toS3Metadata(meta),\n580\t\t})\n581\t\tif err != nil {\n582\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n583\t\t}\n584\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.UploadId), nil\n585\t}\n586\t\n587\t// UploadPart uploads a part to a multipart upload.\n588\tfunc (b *B2Backend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n589\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.UploadPart(ctx, &s3.UploadPartInput{\n590\t\t\tBucket:        aws.String(bucket),\n591\t\t\tKey:           aws.String(key),\n592\t\t\tUploadId:      aws.String(uploadID),\n593\t\t\tPartNumber:    aws.Int32(partNumber),\n594\t\t\tBody:          body,\n595\t\t\tContentLength: CompleteMultipartUpload completes a multipart upload.\n604\tfunc (b *B2Backend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n605\t\t// Convert to AWS SDK type\n606\t\tawsParts := make([]types.CompletedPart, len(parts))\n607\t\tfor i, p := range parts {\n608\t\t\tawsParts[i] = types.CompletedPart{\n609\t\t\t\tETag:       aws.String(p.ETag),\n610\t\t\t\tPartNumber: aws.Int32(p.PartNumber),\n611\t\t\t}\n612\t\t}\n613\t\n614\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput{\n615\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n616\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n617\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n618\t\t\tMultipartUpload: &types.CompletedMultipartUpload{\n619\t\t\t\tParts: awsParts,\n620\t\t\t},\n621\t\t})\n622\t\tif err != nil {\n623\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n624\t\t}\n625\t\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n626\t}\n627\t\n628\t// AbortMultipartUpload aborts a multipart upload.\n629\tfunc (b *B2Backend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n630\t\t_, err := b.s3Client.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput{\n631\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n632\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n633\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n634\t\t})\n635\t\tif err != nil {\n636\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"AbortMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n637\t\t}\n638\t\treturn nil\n639\t}\n640\t\n641\t// ListParts lists the parts of a multipart upload.\n642\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListParts(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) (*ListPartsResult, error) {\n643\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListParts(ctx, &s3.ListPartsInput{\n644\t\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n645\t\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n646\t\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n647\t\t})\n648\t\tif err != nil {\n649\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListParts failed: %w\", err)\n650\t\t}\n651\t\n652\t\tresult := &ListPartsResult{\n653\t\t\tBucket:           aws.ToString(resp.Bucket),\n654\t\t\tKey:              aws.ToString(resp.Key),\n655\t\t\tUploadID:         aws.ToString(resp.UploadId),\n656\t\t\tIsTruncated:      aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\tif resp.NextPartNumberMarker != nil {\n660\t\t\tif val, err := strconv.Atoi(*resp.NextPartNumberMarker); err == nil {\n661\t\t\t\tresult.NextPartNumberMarker = val\n662\t\t\t}\n663\t\t}\n664\t\n665\t\tfor _, part := range resp.Parts {\n666\t\t\tresult.Parts = append(result.Parts, PartInfo{\n667\t\t\t\tPartNumber:   aws.ToInt32(part.PartNumber),\n668\t\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(part.ETag),\n669\t\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(part.Size),\n670\t\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(part.LastModified),\n671\t\t\t})\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\treturn result, nil\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t// ListMultipartUploads lists active multipart uploads.\n678\tfunc (b *B2Backend) ListMultipartUploads(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (*ListMultipartUploadsResult, error) {\n679\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListMultipartUploads(ctx, &s3.ListMultipartUploadsInput{\n680\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n681\t\t})\n682\t\tif err != nil {\n683\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListMultipartUploads failed: %w\", err)\n684\t\t}\n685\t\n686\t\tresult := &ListMultipartUploadsResult{\n687\t\t\tBucket:             aws.ToString(resp.Bucket),\n688\t\t\tIsTruncated:        aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n689\t\t\tNextKeyMarker:      aws.ToString(resp.NextKeyMarker),\n690\t\t\tNextUploadIDMarker: aws.ToString(resp.NextUploadIdMarker),\n691\t\t}\n692\t\n693\t\tfor _, upload := range resp.Uploads {\n694\t\t\tresult.Uploads = append(result.Uploads, UploadInfo{\n695\t\t\t\tUploadID:  aws.ToString(upload.UploadId),\n696\t\t\t\tKey:       aws.ToString(upload.Key),\n697\t\t\t\tInitiated: aws.ToTime(upload.Initiated),\n698\t\t\t})\n699\t\t}\n700\t\n701\t\treturn result, nil\n702\t}\n703\t\n704\t// GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration gets the lifecycle configuration for a bucket.\n705\tfunc (b *B2Backend) GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx context.Context, bucket string) ([]byte, error) {\n706\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx, &s3.GetBucketLifecycleConfigurationInput{\n707\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n708\t\t})\n709\t\tif err != nil {\n710\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration failed: %w\", err)\n711\t\t}\n712\t\n713\t\t// Build the XML response manually to ensure proper S3 format\n714\t\tvar rules []string\n715\t\tfor _, rule := range resp.Rules {\n716\t\t\truleXML := b.buildLifecycleRuleXML(rule)\n717\t\t\trules = append(rules, ruleXML)\n718\t\t}\n719\t\n720\t\txmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(`<LifecycleConfiguration xmlns=\"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\">%s</LifecycleConfiguration>`,\n721\t\t\tstrings.Join(rules, \"\"))\n722\t\treturn []byte(xmlBody), nil\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// buildLifecycleRuleXML builds XML for a single lifecycle rule.\n726\tfunc (b *B2Backend) buildLifecycleRuleXML(rule types.LifecycleRule) string {\n727\t\tvar parts []string\n728\t\n729\t\t// ID\n730\t\tif rule.ID != nil {\n731\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<ID>%s</ID>\", *rule.ID))\n732\t\t}\n733\t\n734\t\t// Status\n735\t\tif rule.Status != \"\" {\n736\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Status>%s</Status>\", rule.Status))\n737\t\t}\n738\t\n739\t\t// Filter\n740\t\tif rule.Filter != nil {\n741\t\t\tfilterXML := b.buildLifecycleFilterXML(rule.Filter)\n742\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Filter>%s</Filter>\", filterXML))\n743\t\t}\n744\t\n745\t\t// Expiration\n746\t\tif rule.Expiration != nil {\n747\t\t\tvar expParts []string\n748\t\t\tif rule.Expiration.Days != nil {\n749\t\t\t\texpParts = append(expParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Days>%d</Days>\", *rule.Expiration.Days))\n750\t\t\t}\n751\t\t\tif rule.Expiration.Date != nil {\n752\t\t\t\texpParts = append(expParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Date>%s</Date>\", rule.Expiration.Date.Format(time.RFC3339)))\n753\t\t\t}\n754\t\t\tif len(expParts) > 0 {\n755\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<Expiration>%s</Expiration>\", strings.Join(expParts, \"\")))\n756\t\t\t}\n757\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\t// NoncurrentVersionExpiration\n760\t\tif rule.NoncurrentVersionExpiration != nil {\n761\t\t\tvar nveParts []string\n762\t\t\tif rule.NoncurrentVersionExpiration.NoncurrentDays != nil {\n763\t\t\t\tnveParts = append(nveParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<NoncurrentDays>%d</NoncurrentDays>\", *rule.NoncurrentVersionExpiration.NoncurrentDays))\n764\t\t\t}\n765\t\t\tif len(nveParts) > 0 {\n766\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<NoncurrentVersionExpiration>%s</NoncurrentVersionExpiration>\", strings.Join(nveParts, \"\")))\n767\t\t\t}\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\t// AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload\n771\t\tif rule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload != nil {\n772\t\t\tvar aimuParts []string\n773\t\t\tif rule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload.DaysAfterInitiation != nil {\n774\t\t\t\taimuParts = append(aimuParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<DaysAfterInitiation>%d</DaysAfterInitiation>\", *rule.AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload.DaysAfterInitiation))\n775\t\t\t}\n776\t\t\tif len(aimuParts) > 0 {\n777\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"<AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload>%s</AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload>\", strings.Join(aimuParts, \"\")))\n778\t\t\t}\n779\t\t}\n780\t\n781\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<Rule>%s</Rule>\", strings.Join(parts, \"\"))\n782\t}\n783\t\n784\t// buildLifecycleFilterXML builds XML for a lifecycle filter.\n785\tfunc (b *B2Backend) buildLifecycleFilterXML(filter *types.LifecycleRuleFilter) string {\n786\t\tif filter == nil {\n787\t\t\treturn \"\"\n788\t\t}\n789\t\n790\t\t// Check which field is set (only one can be set at a time)\n791\t\tif filter.Prefix != *http.Client\n}\n\n// B2Config contains configuration for the B2 backend.\ntype B2Config struct {\n\tRegion      string\n\tEndpoint    string\n\tAccessKeyID string\n\tSecretKey   string\n\tCFDomain    string // Cloudflare domain for free egress downloads\n}\n\n// NewB2Backend creates a new B2 backend.\nfunc NewB2Backend(ctx context.Context, cfg B2Config) (*B2Backend, error) {\n\tawsCfg, err := config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx,\n\t\tconfig.WithRegion(cfg.Region),\n\t\tconfig.WithCredentialsProvider(credentials.NewStaticCredentialsProvider(\n\t\t\tcfg.AccessKeyID,\n\t\t\tcfg.SecretKey,\n\t\t\t\"\",\n\t\t)),\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to load AWS config: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\ts3Client := s3.NewFromConfig(awsCfg, func(o *s3.Options) {\n\t\to.BaseEndpoint = aws.String(cfg.Endpoint)\n\t\to.UsePathStyle = true // B2 requires path-style URLs\n\t\t// SDK >= s3/v1.73 sends CRC32 request checksums by default; B2 then\n\t\t// records checksum-typed multipart parts and rejects a\n\t\t// CompleteMultipartUpload that supplies only ETags with InvalidPart.\n\t\to.RequestChecksumCalculation = aws.RequestChecksumCalculationWhenRequired\n\t\to.ResponseChecksumValidation = aws.ResponseChecksumValidationWhenRequired\n\t})\n\n\treturn &B2Backend{\n\t\ts3Client:   s3Client,\n\t\tregion:     cfg.Region,\n\t\tendpoint:   cfg.Endpoint,\n\t\tcfDomain:   cfg.CFDomain,\n\t\thttpClient: &http.Client{Timeout: 30 * time.Minute},\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// Put stores an object in B2.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) Put(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, body io.Reader, size int64, meta map[string]string) error {\n\t// B2 rejects requests without a Content-Length header. With an unseekable\n\t// body the Go transport falls back to Transfer-Encoding: chunked and drops\n\t// Content-Length (AWS accepts that, B2 does not), so spool to a temp file\n\t// to give the transport a seekable body with a known length.\n\tif _, ok := body.(io.ReadSeeker); !ok {\n\t\ttmp, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-put-*\")\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer os.Remove(tmp.Name())\n\t\tdefer tmp.Close()\n\t\tif _, err := io.Copy(tmp, body); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif _, err := tmp.Seek(0, io.SeekStart); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject spool failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbody = tmp\n\t}\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.PutObject(ctx, &s3.PutObjectInput{\n\t\tBucket:      aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:         aws.String(key),\n\t\tBody:        body,\n\t\tContentLength: aws.Int64(size),\n\t\tMetadata:    toS3Metadata(meta),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PutObject failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Get retrieves an object's full content and metadata.\n// For ARMOR, this uses the Cloudflare domain for free egress.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) Get(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n\t// First get metadata via HeadObject (direct to B2, no egress cost)\n\tinfo, err := b.Head(ctx, bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Fetch body via Cloudflare for free egress\n\tbody, err := b.GetRange(ctx, bucket, key, 0, info.Size)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn body, info, nil\n}\n\n// GetRange retrieves a byte range from an object via Cloudflare.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) GetRange(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, offset, length int64) (io.ReadCloser, error) {\n\tbody, _, err := b.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, bucket, key, offset, length)\n\treturn body, err\n}\n\n// GetRangeWithHeaders retrieves a byte range from an object via Cloudflare along with response headers.\n// Falls back to direct S3 API when cfDomain is empty (e.g. CF CDN unavailable).\nfunc (b *B2Backend) GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, offset, length int64) (io.ReadCloser, map[string]string, error) {\n\tif b.cfDomain == \"\" {\n\t\t// No CF domain configured — fall back to direct S3 range request.\n\t\trangeHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", offset, offset+length-1)\n\t\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{\n\t\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n\t\t\tRange:  aws.String(rangeHeader),\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"direct S3 range request failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn resp.Body, nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Construct Cloudflare download URL\n\tcfURL := fmt.Sprintf(\"https://%s/file/%s/%s\", b.cfDomain, bucket, url.PathEscape(key))\n\n\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, \"GET\", cfURL, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create request: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set Range header\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes=%d-%d\", offset, offset+length-1))\n\n\tresp, err := b.httpClient.Do(req)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cloudflare request failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusPartialContent {\n\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cloudflare returned status %d\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract relevant headers\n\theaders := make(map[string]string)\n\tif cfStatus := resp.Header.Get(\"CF-Cache-Status\"); cfStatus != \"\" {\n\t\theaders[\"CF-Cache-Status\"] = cfStatus\n\t}\n\tif cfRay := resp.Header.Get(\"CF-Ray\"); cfRay != \"\" {\n\t\theaders[\"CF-Ray\"] = cfRay\n\t}\n\tif age := resp.Header.Get(\"Age\"); age != \"\" {\n\t\theaders[\"Age\"] = age\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.Body, headers, nil\n}\n\n// Head retrieves object metadata without the body.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) Head(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HeadObject failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n\t\tKey:          key,\n\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if this is an ARMOR-encrypted object\n\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n\t\t// Use plaintext size from metadata\n\t\tif am, _ := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n\t\t\tinfo.Size = am.PlaintextSize\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn info, nil\n}\n\n// HeadVersion retrieves object metadata for a specific version.\n// Returns ARMOR metadata (plaintext size) for the specific version.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadVersion(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, versionID string) (*ObjectInfo, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.HeadObject(ctx, &s3.HeadObjectInput{\n\t\tBucket:    aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:       aws.String(key),\n\t\tVersionId: aws.String(versionID),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HeadObject failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n\t\tKey:          key,\n\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if this is an ARMOR-encrypted object\n\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n\t\t// Use plaintext size from metadata\n\t\tif am, _ := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n\t\t\tinfo.Size = am.PlaintextSize\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn info, nil\n}\n\n// Delete removes an object from B2.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) Delete(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) error {\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObject(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteObject failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// DeleteObjects removes multiple objects from B2.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteObjects(ctx context.Context, bucket string, keys []string) error {\n\tobjects := make([]types.ObjectIdentifier, len(keys))\n\tfor i, key := range keys {\n\t\tobjects[i] = types.ObjectIdentifier{Key: aws.String(key)}\n\t}\n\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteObjects(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectsInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tDelete: &types.Delete{\n\t\t\tObjects: objects,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteObjects failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// List objects in a bucket with optional prefix.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\n\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n\t\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\n\t\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\n\t}\n\tif delimiter != \"\" {\n\t\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\n\t}\n\tif maxKeys > 0 {\n\t\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\n\t}\n\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &ListResult{\n\t\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n\t\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\n\t}\n\n\t// Process objects\n\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\n\t\t// Filter out .armor/ internal objects\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(aws.ToString(obj.Key), \".armor/\") {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n\t\t\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\n\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\n\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\n\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to get ARMOR metadata (requires additional HeadObject call)\n\t\t// For efficiency in listings, we rely on the plaintext size being\n\t\t// stored in the object's metadata which requires HeadObject per item.\n\t\t// For now, we return the raw size; callers needing plaintext size\n\t\t// should use HeadObject individually.\n\n\t\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\n\t}\n\n\t// Process common prefixes\n\tfor _, prefix := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\n\t\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(prefix.Prefix))\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// Copy copies an object in B2, supporting cross-bucket copy.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) Copy(ctx context.Context, srcBucket, srcKey, dstBucket, dstKey string, meta map[string]string, replaceMetadata bool) error {\n\tinput := &s3.CopyObjectInput{\n\t\tBucket:     aws.String(dstBucket),\n\t\tCopySource: aws.String(srcBucket + \"/\" + srcKey),\n\t\tKey:        aws.String(dstKey),\n\t}\n\n\tif replaceMetadata {\n\t\tinput.MetadataDirective = types.MetadataDirectiveReplace\n\t\tinput.Metadata = toS3Metadata(meta)\n\t}\n\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.CopyObject(ctx, input)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"CopyObject failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GetDirect retrieves an object directly from B2 (not via Cloudflare).\n// Used for metadata operations where we don't need the body.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) GetDirect(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *ObjectInfo, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:    aws.String(key),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"GetObject failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n\t\tKey:          key,\n\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(resp.ContentLength),\n\t\tContentType:  aws.ToString(resp.ContentType),\n\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(resp.ETag),\n\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(resp.LastModified),\n\t\tMetadata:     fromS3Metadata(resp.Metadata),\n\t}\n\n\tif _, ok := ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n\t\tinfo.IsARMOREncrypted = true\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.Body, info, nil\n}\n\n// ComputeETag computes an MD5 ETag for the given data.\nfunc ComputeETag(data []byte) string {\n\thash := md5.Sum(data)\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n}\n\n// ComputeETagReader computes an MD5 ETag from a reader.\nfunc ComputeETagReader(r io.Reader) (string, error) {\n\thash := md5.New()\n\tif _, err := io.Copy(hash, r); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash.Sum(nil)), nil\n}\n\n// toS3Metadata converts a map to S3 metadata format.\nfunc toS3Metadata(meta map[string]string) map[string]string {\n\tif meta == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n\tfor k, v := range meta {\n\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// fromS3Metadata converts S3 metadata to a regular map.\nfunc fromS3Metadata(meta map[string]string) map[string]string {\n\tif meta == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n\tfor k, v := range meta {\n\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ListRaw lists objects without filtering .armor/ internal objects.\n// Used for internal operations such as manifest discovery where all keys\n// under a prefix (including .armor/ prefixed keys) must be enumerated.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) ListRaw(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*ListResult, error) {\n\tinput := &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{\n\t\tBucket:            aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tPrefix:            aws.String(prefix),\n\t\tContinuationToken: aws.String(continuationToken),\n\t}\n\tif delimiter != \"\" {\n\t\tinput.Delimiter = aws.String(delimiter)\n\t}\n\tif maxKeys > 0 {\n\t\tinput.MaxKeys = aws.Int32(int32(maxKeys))\n\t}\n\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, input)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListObjectsV2 failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &ListResult{\n\t\tIsTruncated: aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n\t\tNextToken:   aws.ToString(resp.NextContinuationToken),\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, obj := range resp.Contents {\n\t\tinfo := &ObjectInfo{\n\t\t\tKey:          aws.ToString(obj.Key),\n\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(obj.Size),\n\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(obj.ETag),\n\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(obj.LastModified),\n\t\t}\n\t\tresult.Objects = append(result.Objects, *info)\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, p := range resp.CommonPrefixes {\n\t\tresult.CommonPrefixes = append(result.CommonPrefixes, aws.ToString(p.Prefix))\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// S3Client returns the underlying S3 client for advanced operations.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) S3Client() *s3.Client {\n\treturn b.s3Client\n}\n\n// IsRetryableError checks if an error is retryable.\nfunc IsRetryableError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\t// Check for common retryable conditions\n\terrStr := err.Error()\n\treturn strings.Contains(errStr, \"5xx\") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"503\") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"502\") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"timeout\") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(errStr, \"connection reset\")\n}\n\n// ListBuckets lists all buckets.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) ListBuckets(ctx context.Context) ([]BucketInfo, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListBuckets(ctx, &s3.ListBucketsInput{})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListBuckets failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tbuckets := make([]BucketInfo, len(resp.Buckets))\n\tfor i, bucket := range resp.Buckets {\n\t\tbuckets[i] = BucketInfo{\n\t\t\tName:         aws.ToString(bucket.Name),\n\t\t\tCreationDate: aws.ToTime(bucket.CreationDate),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn buckets, nil\n}\n\n// CreateBucket creates a new bucket.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.CreateBucket(ctx, &s3.CreateBucketInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"CreateBucket failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// DeleteBucket deletes an empty bucket.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) DeleteBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.DeleteBucket(ctx, &s3.DeleteBucketInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"DeleteBucket failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// HeadBucket checks if a bucket exists.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) HeadBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.HeadBucket(ctx, &s3.HeadBucketInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"HeadBucket failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ReaderAtCloser wraps a reader with Close and ReadAt methods.\ntype ReaderAtCloser struct {\n\tdata []byte\n}\n\n// NewReaderAtCloser creates a ReaderAtCloser from a byte slice.\nfunc NewReaderAtCloser(data []byte) *ReaderAtCloser {\n\treturn &ReaderAtCloser{data: data}\n}\n\nfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\n\tif len(r.data) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0, io.EOF\n\t}\n\tn = copy(p, r.data)\n\tr.data = r.data[n:]\n\treturn n, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) ReadAt(p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error) {\n\tif off >= int64(len(r.data)) {\n\t\treturn 0, io.EOF\n\t}\n\tn = copy(p, r.data[off:])\n\tif n < len(p) {\n\t\terr = io.EOF\n\t}\n\treturn n, err\n}\n\nfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Close() error {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *ReaderAtCloser) Bytes() []byte {\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// CreateMultipartUpload initiates a multipart upload.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput{\n\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n\t\tMetadata: toS3Metadata(meta),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn aws.ToString(resp.UploadId), nil\n}\n\n// UploadPart uploads a part to a multipart upload.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.UploadPart(ctx, &s3.UploadPartInput{\n\t\tBucket:        aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:           aws.String(key),\n\t\tUploadId:      aws.String(uploadID),\n\t\tPartNumber:    aws.Int32(partNumber),\n\t\tBody:          body,\n\t\tContentLength: aws.Int64(size),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"UploadPart failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n}\n\n// CompleteMultipartUpload completes a multipart upload.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n\t// Convert to AWS SDK type\n\tawsParts := make([]types.CompletedPart, len(parts))\n\tfor i, p := range parts {\n\t\tawsParts[i] = types.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tETag:       aws.String(p.ETag),\n\t\t\tPartNumber: aws.Int32(p.PartNumber),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CompleteMultipartUploadInput{\n\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n\t\tMultipartUpload: &types.CompletedMultipartUpload{\n\t\t\tParts: awsParts,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn aws.ToString(resp.ETag), nil\n}\n\n// AbortMultipartUpload aborts a multipart upload.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n\t_, err := b.s3Client.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.AbortMultipartUploadInput{\n\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"AbortMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ListParts lists the parts of a multipart upload.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) ListParts(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) (*ListPartsResult, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListParts(ctx, &s3.ListPartsInput{\n\t\tBucket:   aws.String(bucket),\n\t\tKey:      aws.String(key),\n\t\tUploadId: aws.String(uploadID),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListParts failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &ListPartsResult{\n\t\tBucket:           aws.ToString(resp.Bucket),\n\t\tKey:              aws.ToString(resp.Key),\n\t\tUploadID:         aws.ToString(resp.UploadId),\n\t\tIsTruncated:      aws.ToBool(resp.IsTruncated),\n\t}\n\n\tif resp.NextPartNumberMarker != nil {\n\t\tif val, err := strconv.Atoi(*resp.NextPartNumberMarker); err == nil {\n\t\t\tresult.NextPartNumberMarker = val\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range resp.Parts {\n\t\tresult.Parts = append(result.Parts, PartInfo{\n\t\t\tPartNumber:   aws.ToInt32(part.PartNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:         aws.ToString(part.ETag),\n\t\t\tSize:         aws.ToInt64(part.Size),\n\t\t\tLastModified: aws.ToTime(part.LastModified),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// ListMultipartUploads lists active multipart uploads.\nfunc (b *B2Backend) ListMultipartUploads(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (*ListMultipartUploadsResult, error) {\n\tresp, err := b.s3Client.ListMultipartUploads(ctx, &s3.ListMultipartUploadsInput{\n\t\tBucket: aws.String(bucket),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ListMultipartUploads failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &ListMultipartUploadsResult{\n\t\tBucket: \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Check which field is set (only one can be set at a time)\n\tif filter.Prefix != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<Prefix>%s</Prefix>\", *filter.Prefix)\n\t}\n\tif filter.Tag != nil Error Handling and Context Propagation Patterns\n\n### Error Handling Pattern\nAll operations follow consistent error handling:\n```go\n// Always use fmt.Errorf with %w for error wrapping\nif err != nil {\n    return \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: %w\", err)\n}\n\n// Include context in error messages\nreturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to load multipart state: %w\", err)\n```\n\n### Context Propagation Pattern\nAll methods accept `context.Context` as the first parameter:\n```go\nfunc (b *B2Backend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n    resp, err := b.s3Client.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, &s3.CreateMultipartUploadInput{\n        // Context is passed through to AWS SDK operations\n    })\n}\n```\n\n### Retry Logic\nThe package includes retry detection:\n```go\nfunc IsRetryableError(err error) bool {\n    if err == nil {\n        return false\n    }\n    errStr := err.Error()\n    return strings.Contains(errStr, \"5xx\") ||\n        strings.Contains(errStr, \"503\") ||\n        strings.Contains(errStr, \"timeout\")\n}\n```\n\n### ARMOR-Specific Error Handling\nARMOR metadata parsing with proper error handling:\n```go\nfunc ParseARMORMetadata(meta map[string]string) (*ARMORMetadata, bool) {\n    version := meta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-version\"]\n    if version == \"\" {\n        return nil, false\n    }\n    // Parse and return ARMOR metadata\n}\n```\n\n## 5. Key Implementation Patterns\n\n### Metadata Management\n- ARMOR metadata stored in S3 user metadata headers with `x-amz-meta-armor-` prefix\n- Base64 encoding for binary data (IV, wrapped DEK)\n- Functions for converting between ARMOR and S3 metadata formats\n\n### Testing Infrastructure\n- Mock backend implementation in test files for testing without real S3 operations\n- Interface-based design allows easy mocking\n- Test helpers for common multipart scenarios\n\n### Caching Strategy\n- LRU cache for metadata and list results\n- TTL-based expiration\n- Thread-safe with proper locking\n\n### Constants and Limits\nStandard S3 multipart limits defined:\n- Minimum part size: 5MB\n- Maximum part size: 5GB  \n- Maximum number of parts: 10,000\n- Maximum object size: 5TB\n\nThis architecture provides a clean, extensible foundation for adding new S3 operations while maintaining consistency with existing patterns.","usage":{"total_tokens":0,"tool_uses":19,"duration_ms":57719},"uuid":"568e637f-1b8e-4255-b65e-f5497df5f7c5","session_id":"7f9d5688-343a-4f89-ba6a-0beadae98d55"}

(h *MultipartUploadHelper) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo, partNumber int, data []byte) error {\n\tif partNumber < 1 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid part number: %d (must files.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) UploadPartsInSeries(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo, partSize int, data []byte) error {\n\tif len(data) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no data file size.\n// It aims to use parts between 10MB and 100MB for efficiency while staying within limits.\nfunc CalculateOptimalPartSize(fileSize int64) int64 {\n\tconst (\n\t\tminPartSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024  // 10MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize = 100 * 1024 * 1024 // 100MB\n\t\tmaxParts    = 10000\n\t)\n\n\t// For small files, use the minimum part size\n\tif fileSize <= minPartSize*maxParts {\n\t\treturn minPartSize\n\t}\n\n\t// For larger files, calculate a part size that keeps us under maxParts\n\tpartSize := fileSize / maxParts\n\tif partSize < minPartSize {\n\t\tpartSize = minPartSize\n\t}\n\tif MultipartUploadInfo holds information about an in-progress multipart upload.\ntype MultipartUploadInfo struct {\n\tUploadID string\n\tKey      string\n\tParts    []CompletedPart\n\tSize     int64\n}\n\n// CreateUpload creates a new multipart upload with the given key and metadata.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) CreateUpload(ctx context.Context, key string, metadata map[string]string) (*MultipartUploadInfo, error) {\n\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, h.bucket, key, metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create multipart upload: multipart upload.\n// The part number must be >= 1 and parts must be uploaded in order.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo, partNumber int, data []byte) error {\n\tif partNumber < 1 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid part number: %d (must be >= 1)\", partNumber)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check minimum part size (5MB) unless this is the first/only final ETag.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) CompleteUpload(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo) (string, error) {\n\tif len(info.Parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"cannot multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// UploadPartsInSeries uploads multiple parts in series (one after another).\n// This is simpler than parallel uploads but slower for large files.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) UploadPartsInSeries(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo, partSize int, data []byte) error {\n\tif len(data) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no data expected %d)\", objInfo.Size, len(data))\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up\n\terr = h.backend.Delete(ctx, h.bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary cleanup failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// VerifyMultipartUpload uploads data via multipart and downloads it back to verify integrity.\n// This is useful for canary monitors to verify the full multipart upload code path.\nfunc VerifyMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, backend Backend, bucket, key string, data []byte, partSize int) error {\n\tif len(data) < 5*1024*1024 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"data size must be at least 5MB for multipart upload\")\n\t}\n\n\tif partSize < 5*1024*1024 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"part size must be at least 5MB\")\n\t}\n\n\thelper := NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend,         = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\n\t)\n\n\treturn MultipartUploadConstants{\n\t\tMinPartSize:            minPartSize,\n\t\tMaxPartSize:            maxPartSize,\n\t\tMaxPartsCount: file size.\n// It aims to use parts between 10MB and 100MB for efficiency while staying within limits.\nfunc CalculateOptimalPartSize(fileSize int64) int64 {\n\tconst (\n\t\tminPartSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024  // 10MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize = 100 * 1024 * 1024 // 100MB\n\t\tmaxParts    = 10000\n\t)\n\n\t// For small files, use the minimum part size\n\tif fileSize <= minPartSize*maxParts {\n\t\treturn minPartSize\n\t}\n\n\t// For larger files, calculate a part size that keeps us under maxParts\n\tpartSize := fileSize / maxParts\n\tif partSize < minPartSize {\n\t\tpartSize = minPartSize\n\t}\n\tif
